If events keep\\n57: \\t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\\n58: \\tdebounceMax time.Duration\\n59: \\n60: \\t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\\n61: \\tenableEDSDebounce bool\\n62: }\\n63: \\n64: // DiscoveryServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\\n65: type DiscoveryServer struct {\\n66: \\t// Env is the model environment.\\n67: \\tEnv *model.Environment\\n68: \\n69: \\t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\\n70: \\t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\\n71: \\t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\\n72: \\t// different generator for a type.\\n73: \\t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\\n74: \\tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\\n75: \\n76: \\t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators\\n77: \\t// may also choose to not send any updates.\\n78: \\tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\\n79: \\n80: \\t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\\n81: \\tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\\n82: \\t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\\n83: \\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\\n84: \\n85: \\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\\n86: \\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\\n87: \\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\\n88: \\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\\n89: \\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\\n90: \\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\\n91: \\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\\n92: \\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\\n93: \\n94: \\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\\n95: \\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\\n96: \\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\\n97: \\n98: \\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\\n99: \\tpushQueue *PushQueue\\n100: \\n101: \\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\\n102: \\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\\n103: \\n104: \\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\\n105: \\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\\n106: \\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\\n107: \\n108: \\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. Should be same/subset of the CA authenticators.\\n109: \\tAuthenticators []security.Authenticator\\n110: \\n111: \\tWorkloadEntryController *autoregistration.Controller\\n112: \\n113: \\t// serverReady indicates caches have been synced up and server is ready to process requests.\\n114: \\tserverReady atomic.Bool\\n115: \\n116: \\tDebounceOptions DebounceOptions\\n117: \\n118: \\t// Cache for XDS resources\\n119: \\tCache model.XdsCache\\n120: \\n121: \\t// JwtKeyResolver holds a reference to the JWT key resolver instance.\\n122: \\tJwtKeyResolver *model.JwksResolver\\n123: \\n124: \\t// ListRemoteClusters collects debug information about other clusters this istiod reads from.\\n125: \\tListRemoteClusters func() []cluster.DebugInfo\\n126: \\n127: \\t// ClusterAliases are alias names for cluster. When a proxy connects with a cluster ID\\n128: \\t// and if it has a different alias we should use that a cluster ID for proxy.\\n129: \\tClusterAliases map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID\\n130: \\n131: \\t// pushVersion stores the numeric push version. This should be accessed via NextVersion()\\n132: \\tpushVersion atomic.Uint64\\n133: \\n134: \\t// DiscoveryStartTime is the time since the binary started\\n135: \\tDiscoveryStartTime time.Time\\n136: \\n137: \\tkrtDebugger *krt.DebugHandler\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: // NewDiscoveryServer creates DiscoveryServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures\\n141: func NewDiscoveryServer(env *model.Environment, clusterAliases map[string]string, debugger *krt.DebugHandler) *DiscoveryServer {\\n142: \\tout := &DiscoveryServer{\\n143: \\t\\tEnv:                 env,\\n144: \\t\\tGenerators:          map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator{},\\n145: \\t\\tProxyNeedsPush:      DefaultProxyNeedsPush,\\n146: \\t\\tconcurrentPushLimit: make(chan struct{}, features.PushThrottle),\\n147: \\t\\tRequestRateLimit:    rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(features.RequestLimit), 1),\\n148: \\t\\tInboundUpdates:      atomic.NewInt64(0),\\n149: \\t\\tCommittedUpdates: \\t\\tout.ClusterAliases[cluster.ID(alias)] = cluster.ID(clusterAliases[alias])\\n167: \\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\tout.initJwksResolver()\\n170: \\n171: \\treturn out\\n172: }\\n173: \\n174: // initJwkResolver initializes the JWT key resolver to be used.\\n175: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initJwksResolver() {\\n176: \\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\\n177: \\t\\ts.closeJwksResolver()\\n178: \\t}\\n179: \\ts.JwtKeyResolver = model.NewJwksResolver(\\n180: \\t\\tmodel.JwtPubKeyEvictionDuration, model.JwtPubKeyRefreshInterval,\\n181: \\t\\tmodel.JwtPubKeyRefreshIntervalOnFailure, the ADS handler to the grpc server\\n197: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\\n198: \\t// Register v3 server\\n199: \\tdiscovery.RegisterAggregatedDiscoveryServiceServer(rpcs, s)\\n200: }\\n201: \\n202: var processStartTime = time.Now()\\n203: \\n204: // CachesSynced is called when caches have been synced so that server can accept connections.\\n205: func (s *DiscoveryServer) CachesSynced() {\\n206: \\tlog.Infof(\\\"All caches have been synced up in %v, marking server ready\\\", time.Since(s.DiscoveryStartTime))\\n207: \\ts.serverReady.Store(true)\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: func (s *DiscoveryServer) IsServerReady() bool {\\n211: \\treturn s.serverReady.Load()\\n212: }\\n213: \\n214: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n215: \\tgo }\\n221: \\n222: // Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\\n223: func (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n224: \\tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\\n225: \\tdefer ticker.Stop()\\n226: \\tfor {\\n227: \\t\\tselect {\\n228: \\t\\tcase <-ticker.C:\\n229: \\t\\t\\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\\n230: \\t\\t\\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\\n231: \\t\\t\\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\\n234: \\t\\t\\t\\tout, _ \\t\\t\\t}\\n239: \\t\\t\\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\\n240: \\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n241: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n242: \\t\\t}\\n243: \\t}\\n244: }\\n245: \\n246: // dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request\\n247: func (s *DiscoveryServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n248: \\t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\\n249: \\tif req.Forced {\\n250: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n251: \\t} else {\\n252: \\t\\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\\n253: \\t\\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\\n254: \\t}\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\\n258: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n259: \\tif !req.Full {\\n260: \\t\\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\\n261: \\t\\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\\n262: \\t\\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\\n263: \\t\\treturn\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\t// Reset the status during the push.\\n266: \\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\\n267: \\tif oldPushContext != nil {\\n268: \\t\\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\\n269: \\t\\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\\n270: \\t\\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\\n271: \\t}\\n272: \\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\\n273: \\t// saved.\\n274: \\tt0 := time.Now()\\n275: \\tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\\n276: \\tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\\n277: \\tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\\n278: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"InitContext %v for push took %s\\\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\\n279: \\tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\\n280: \\n281: \\treq.Push = push\\n282: \\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\\n283: }\\n284: \\n285: func nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\\n286: \\treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: // Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\\n290: // PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\\n291: // only be used for \\\"global\\\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\\n292: func (s *DiscoveryServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\\n293: \\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\\n294: }\\n295: \\n296: var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\\\"fullpush\\\", \\\"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\\\")\\n297: \\n298: // ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n300: \\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n301: \\t\\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\\n302: \\t\\t\\tpanic(\\\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\\\")\\n303: \\t\\t}\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\\n306: \\t\\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\\n307: \\t\\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\\n308: \\t\\t// the cache.\\n309: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\\n312: \\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\\n313: \\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\\n314: \\t\\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\\n315: \\t\\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\\n316: \\t\\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\\\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\\\", configs, reasons)\\n317: \\t}\\n318: \\ts.pushChannel <- req\\n319: }\\n320: \\n321: // Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\\n322: // and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\\n323: // handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\\n324: // It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\\n325: // It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\\n326: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n327: \\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\\n328: }\\n329: \\n330: // The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\\n331: func debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\\n332: \\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\\n333: \\tvar startDebounce time.Time\\n334: \\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\\n335: \\n336: \\tpushCounter := 0\\n337: \\tdebouncedEvents := 0\\n338: \\n339: \\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\\n340: \\tvar req *model.PushRequest\\n341: \\n342: \\tfree := true\\n343: \\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\\n344: \\n345: \\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\\n346: \\t\\tpushFn(req)\\n347: \\t\\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\\n348: \\t\\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\\n349: \\t\\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\\n350: \\t}\\n351: \\n352: \\tpushWorker := func() {\\n353: \\t\\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\\n354: \\t\\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\\n355: \\t\\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\\n356: \\t\\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\\n357: \\t\\t\\tif req != nil {\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter++\\n359: \\t\\t\\t\\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\\\",\\n361: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\\n362: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\\\",\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\tfree = false\\n369: \\t\\t\\t\\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\treq = nil\\n371: \\t\\t\\t\\tdebouncedEvents = 0\\n372: \\t\\t\\t}\\n373: \\t\\t} else {\\n374: \\t\\t\\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\\n375: \\t\\t}\\n376: \\t}\\n377: \\n378: \\tfor {\\n379: \\t\\tselect {\\n380: \\t\\tcase <-freeCh:\\n381: \\t\\t\\tfree = true\\n382: \\t\\t\\tpushWorker()\\n383: \\t\\tcase r := <-ch:\\n384: \\t\\t\\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\\n385: \\t\\t\\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\\n387: \\t\\t\\t}\\n388: \\t\\t\\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\\n389: \\t\\t\\t\\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\tgo func(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n391: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushFn(req)\\n392: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tupdateSent.Inc()\\n393: \\t\\t\\t\\t}(r)\\n394: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n395: \\t\\t\\t}\\n396: \\n397: \\t\\t\\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\\n398: \\t\\t\\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\\n401: \\t\\t\\t}\\n402: \\t\\t\\tdebouncedEvents++\\n403: \\n404: \\t\\t\\treq = req.Merge(r)\\n405: \\t\\tcase <-timeChan:\\n406: \\t\\t\\tif free {\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushWorker()\\n408: \\t\\t\\t}\\n409: \\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n410: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n411: \\t\\t}\\n412: \\t}\\n413: }\\n414: \\n415: func configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\\n416: \\tconfigs := \\\"\\\"\\n417: \\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\\n418: \\t\\tconfigs += key.String()\\n419: \\t\\tbreak\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\\n422: \\t\\tmore := \\\" and \\\" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \\\" more configs\\\"\\n423: \\t\\tconfigs += more\\n424: \\t}\\n425: \\treturn configs\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: func reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\\n429: \\tvar (\\n430: \\t\\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\\n431: \\t\\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\\n432: \\t)\\n433: \\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\\n434: \\t\\tif idx == 0 {\\n435: \\t\\t\\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\\n436: \\t\\t} else if idx == 1 {\\n437: \\t\\t\\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\\n438: \\t\\t} else {\\n439: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n440: \\t\\t}\\n441: \\t\\tidx++\\n442: \\t}\\n443: \\n444: \\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\\n445: \\tcase 0:\\n446: \\t\\treturn \\\"unknown\\\"\\n447: \\tcase 1:\\n448: \\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s:%d\\\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\\n449: \\tcase 2:\\n450: \\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s:%d and %s:%d\\\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\\n451: \\tdefault:\\n452: \\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\\\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\\n453: \\t\\t\\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\\n454: \\t}\\n455: }\\n456: \\n457: func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\\n458: \\tfor {\\n459: \\t\\tselect {\\n460: \\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n461: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n462: \\t\\tdefault:\\n463: \\t\\t\\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\\n464: \\t\\t\\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\\n465: \\t\\t\\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\\n466: \\n467: \\t\\t\\t// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.\\n468: \\t\\t\\tclient, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()\\n469: \\t\\t\\tif shuttingdown {\\n470: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n471: \\t\\t\\t}\\n472: \\t\\t\\trecordPushTriggers(push.Reason)\\n473: \\t\\t\\t// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.\\n474: \\t\\t\\tdoneFunc := func() {\\n475: \\t\\t\\t\\tqueue.MarkDone(client)\\n476: \\t\\t\\t\\t<-semaphore\\n477: \\t\\t\\t}\\n478: \\n479: \\t\\t\\tproxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())\\n480: \\t\\t\\tvar closed <-chan struct{}\\n481: \\t\\t\\tif client.deltaStream != nil {\\n482: \\t\\t\\t\\tclosed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()\\n483: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n484: \\t\\t\\t\\tclosed = client.StreamDone()\\n485: \\t\\t\\t}\\n486: \\t\\t\\tgo func() {\\n487: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushEv := &Event{\\n488: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushRequest: push,\\n489: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdone:        doneFunc,\\n490: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n491: \\n492: \\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n493: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase client.PushCh() <- pushEv:\\n494: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n495: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-closed: // grpc stream was closed\\n496: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdoneFunc()\\n497: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Client closed connection %v\\\", client.ID())\\n498: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n499: \\t\\t\\t}()\\n500: \\t\\t}\\n501: \\t}\\n502: }\\n503: \\n504: // initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this\\n505: // method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\\n506: // if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\\n507: // reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\\n508: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\\n509: \\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\\n510: \\tpush.PushVersion = version\\n511: \\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\\n512: \\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\\n513: \\n514: \\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\\n515: \\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\\n516: \\n517: \\treturn push\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n521: \\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\\n522: }\\n523: \\n524: // Shutdown shuts down DiscoveryServer components.\\n525: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Shutdown() {\\n526: \\ts.closeJwksResolver()\\n527: \\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\\n528: }\\n529: \\n530: // Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\\n531: // but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\\n532: // This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\\n533: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {\\n534: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n535: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n536: \\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\\n537: \\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\\n538: \\t\\tselect {\\n539: \\t\\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\\n540: \\t\\tdefault:\\n541: \\t\\t\\t// Initialization not complete, skip\\n542: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n543: \\t\\t}\\n544: \\t\\tclients = append(clients, con)\\n545: \\t}\\n546: \\treturn clients\\n547: }\\n548: \\n549: // SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\\n550: // Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\\n551: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\\n552: \\tclients := s.Clients()\\n553: \\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\\n554: \\t\\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\\n555: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n556: \\t\\t}\\n557: \\t\\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\\n558: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n559: \\t\\t}\\n560: \\t\\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\\n561: \\t})\\n562: \\treturn clients\\n563: }\\n564: \\n565: // AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\\n566: // Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\\n567: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\\n568: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n569: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n570: \\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\\n571: }\\n572: \\n573: func (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\\n574: \\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\\n575: \\t\\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\\n576: \\t\\treturn nil\\n577: \\t}\\n578: \\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\\n579: \\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\\n580: \\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\\n581: \\tdefer cancel()\\n582: \\treturn events to wait\\n48: \\t// after a registry/config event for debouncing.\\n49: \\t// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus\\n50: \\t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\\n51: \\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\\n52: \\t// delaying until things settle.\\n53: \\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\\n54: \\n55: \\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\\n56: \\t// while debouncing. Individual generators\\n77: \\t// may also choose to not send any updates.\\n78: \\tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\\n79: \\n80: \\t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\\n81: \\tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\\n82: \\t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\\n83: \\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\\n84: \\n85: \\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\\n86: \\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\\n87: \\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\\n88: \\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\\n89: \\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\\n90: \\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\\n91: \\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\\n92: \\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\\n93: \\n94: \\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\\n95: \\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\\n96: \\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\\n97: \\n98: \\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\\n99: \\tpushQueue *PushQueue\\n100: \\n101: \\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\\n102: \\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\\n103: \\n104: \\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\\n105: \\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\\n106: \\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\\n107: \\n108: \\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. This should\\n291: // only be used for \\\"global\\\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\\n292: func (s *DiscoveryServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\\n293: \\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\\n294: }\\n295: \\n296: var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\\\"fullpush\\\", \\\"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\\\")\\n297: \\n298: // ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n300: \\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n301: \\t\\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\\n302: \\t\\t\\tpanic(\\\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\\\")\\n303: \\t\\t}\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\\n306: \\t\\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\\n340: \\tvar req *model.PushRequest\\n341: \\n342: \\tfree := true\\n343: \\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\\n344: \\n345: \\tpush := func(req \\t\\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\\n349: \\t\\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\\n350: \\t}\\n351: \\n352: \\tpushWorker := func() {\\n353: \\t\\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\\n354: \\t\\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\\n355: \\t\\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\\n356: \\t\\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\\n357: \\t\\t\\tif req != nil {\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter++\\n359: \\t\\t\\t\\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d req.Full)\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\\\",\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\tfree = false\\n369: \\t\\t\\t\\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\treq = nil\\n371: \\t\\t\\t\\tdebouncedEvents = 0\\n372: \\t\\t\\t}\\n373: \\t\\t} else {\\n374: \\t\\t\\ttimeChan = \\t\\t\\tpushWorker()\\n383: \\t\\tcase r := <-ch:\\n384: \\t\\t\\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\\n385: \\t\\t\\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\\n387: \\t\\t\\t}\\n388: \\t\\t\\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\\n389: \\t\\t\\t\\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\tgo func(req \\t\\t\\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\\n398: \\t\\t\\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\\n401: \\t\\t\\t}\\n402: \\t\\t\\tdebouncedEvents++\\n403: \\n404: \\t\\t\\treq = req.Merge(r)\\n405: \\t\\tcase <-timeChan:\\n406: \\t\\t\\tif free {\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushWorker()\\n408: \\t\\t\\t}\\n409: \\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n410: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n411: \\t\\t}\\n412: \\t}\\n413: }\\n414: \\n415: func configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\\n416: \\tconfigs := \\\"\\\"\\n417: \\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\\n418: \\t\\tconfigs += key.String()\\n419: \\t\\tbreak\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\\n422: \\t\\tmore := \\\" and \\\" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \\\" more configs\\\"\\n423: \\t\\tconfigs += more\\n424: \\t}\\n425: \\treturn configs\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: func reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\\n429: \\tvar (\\n430: \\t\\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\\n431: \\t\\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\\n432: \\t)\\n433: \\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\\n434: \\t\\tif idx == 0 {\\n435: \\t\\t\\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\\n436: \\t\\t} else if idx == 1 {\\n437: \\t\\t\\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\\n438: \\t\\t} else {\\n439: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n440: \\t\\t}\\n441: \\t\\tidx++\\n442: \\t}\\n443: \\n444: \\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\\n445: \\tcase 0:\\n446: \\t\\treturn \\\"unknown\\\"\\n447: \\tcase 1:\\n448: \\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s:%d\\\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\\n449: \\tcase 2:\\n450: \\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s:%d and %s:%d\\\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\\n451: \\tdefault:\\n452: \\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\\\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\\n453: \\t\\t\\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\\n454: \\t}\\n455: }\\n456: \\n457: func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\\n458: \\tfor {\\n459: \\t\\tselect {\\n460: \\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n461: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n462: \\t\\tdefault:\\n463: \\t\\t\\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\\n464: \\t\\t\\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\\n465: \\t\\t\\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\\n466: \\n467: \\t\\t\\t// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.\\n468: \\t\\t\\tclient, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()\\n469: \\t\\t\\tif shuttingdown {\\n470: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n471: \\t\\t\\t}\\n472: \\t\\t\\trecordPushTriggers(push.Reason)\\n473: \\t\\t\\t// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.\\n474: \\t\\t\\tdoneFunc := func() {\\n475: \\t\\t\\t\\tqueue.MarkDone(client)\\n476: \\t\\t\\t\\t<-semaphore\\n477: \\t\\t\\t}\\n478: \\n479: \\t\\t\\tproxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())\\n480: \\t\\t\\tvar closed <-chan struct{}\\n481: \\t\\t\\tif client.deltaStream != nil {\\n482: \\t\\t\\t\\tclosed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()\\n483: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n484: \\t\\t\\t\\tclosed = client.StreamDone()\\n485: \\t\\t\\t}\\n486: \\t\\t\\tgo func() {\\n487: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushEv := &Event{\\n488: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushRequest: push,\\n489: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdone:        doneFunc,\\n490: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n491: \\n492: \\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n493: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase client.PushCh() <- pushEv:\\n494: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n495: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-closed: // grpc stream was closed\\n496: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdoneFunc()\\n497: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Client closed connection %v\\\", client.ID())\\n498: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n499: \\t\\t\\t}()\\n500: \\t\\t}\\n501: \\t}\\n502: }\\n503: \\n504: // initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this\\n505: // method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\\n506: // if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\\n507: // reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\\n508: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\\n509: \\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\\n510: \\tpush.PushVersion = version\\n511: \\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\\n512: \\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\\n513: \\n514: \\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\\n515: \\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\\n516: \\n517: \\treturn push\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n521: \\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\\n522: }\\n523: \\n524: // Shutdown shuts down DiscoveryServer components.\\n525: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Shutdown() {\\n526: \\ts.closeJwksResolver()\\n527: \\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\\n528: }\\n529: \\n530: // Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\\n531: // but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\\n532: // This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\\n533: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {\\n534: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n535: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n536: \\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\\n537: \\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\\n538: \\t\\tselect {\\n539: \\t\\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\\n540: \\t\\tdefault:\\n541: \\t\\t\\t// Initialization not complete, skip\\n542: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n543: \\t\\t}\\n544: \\t\\tclients = append(clients, con)\\n545: \\t}\\n546: \\treturn clients\\n547: }\\n548: \\n549: // SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\\n550: // Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\\n551: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\\n552: \\tclients := s.Clients()\\n553: \\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\\n554: \\t\\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\\n555: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n556: \\t\\t}\\n557: \\t\\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\\n558: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n559: \\t\\t}\\n560: \\t\\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\\n561: \\t})\\n562: \\treturn clients\\n563: }\\n564: \\n565: // AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\\n566: // Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\\n567: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\\n568: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n569: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n570: \\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\\n571: }\\n572: \\n573: func (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\\n574: \\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\\n575: \\t\\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\\n576: \\t\\treturn nil\\n577: \\t}\\n578: \\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\\n579: \\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\\n580: \\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\\n581: \\tdefer cancel()\\n582: \\treturn time.Second\n45: \n46: type DebounceOptions struct {\n47: \t// DebounceAfter is the delay added to events to wait\n48: \t// after a registry/config event for debouncing.\n49: \t// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus\n50: \t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\n51: \t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n52: \t// delaying until things settle.\n53: \tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n54: \n55: \t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n56: \t// while debouncing. If events keep\n57: \t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\n58: \tdebounceMax time.Duration\n59: \n60: \t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\n61: \tenableEDSDebounce bool\n62: }\n63: \n64: // DiscoveryServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\n65: type DiscoveryServer struct {\n66: \t// Env is the model environment.\n67: \tEnv *model.Environment\n68: \n69: \t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\n70: \t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\n71: \t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\n72: \t// different generator for a type.\n73: \t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\n74: \tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\n75: \n76: \t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n83: \tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n84: \n85: \t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n86: \tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n87: \t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n88: \t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\n89: \t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\n90: \t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\n91: \t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\n92: \tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\n93: \n94: \t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\n95: \t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\n96: \tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\n97: \n98: \t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\n99: \tpushQueue *PushQueue\n100: \n101: \t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\n102: \tdebugHandlers map[string]string\n103: \n104: \t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\n105: \tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\n106: \tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\n107: \n108: \t// Authenticators for XDS requests. // Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\n223: func (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n224: \tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\n225: \tdefer ticker.Stop()\n226: \tfor {\n227: \t\tselect {\n228: \t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n229: \t\t\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\n230: \t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n231: \t\t\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\n232: \t\t\t\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\n233: \t\t\t\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\n234: \t\t\t\tout, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n235: \t\t\t\tif string(out) != \"{}\" {\n236: \t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push Status: %s\", string(out))\n237: \t\t\t\t}\n238: \t\t\t}\n239: \t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n240: \t\tcase <-stopCh:\n241: \t\t\treturn\n242: \t\t}\n243: \t}\n244: }\n245: \n246: // dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request\n247: func (s *DiscoveryServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\n248: \t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\n249: \tif req.Forced {\n250: \t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n251: \t} else {\n252: \t\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\n253: \t\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\n254: \t}\n255: }\n256: \n257: // Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\n258: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\n259: \tif !req.Full {\n260: \t\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\n261: \t\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n262: \t\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n263: \t\treturn\n264: \t}\n265: \t// Reset the status during the push.\n266: \toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\n267: \tif oldPushContext != nil {\n268: \t\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\n269: \t\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\n270: \t\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\n271: \t}\n272: \t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\n273: \t// saved.\n274: \tt0 := time.Now()\n275: \tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\n276: \tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\n277: \tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\n278: \tlog.Debugf(\"InitContext %v for push took %s\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\n279: \tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\n280: \n281: \treq.Push = push\n282: \ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n283: }\n284: \n285: func nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\n286: \treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\n287: }\n288: \n289: // Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\n290: // PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\n291: // only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\n292: func (s *DiscoveryServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n293: \treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n294: }\n295: \n296: var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n297: \n298: // ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n300: \tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n301: \t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n302: \t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n303: \t\t}\n304: \t}\n305: \tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n306: \t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n307: \t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n308: \t\t// the cache.\n309: \t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n310: \t}\n311: \tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n312: \ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n313: \tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n314: \t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n315: \t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n316: \t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n317: \t}\n318: \ts.pushChannel <- req\n319: }\n320: \n321: // Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n322: // and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n323: // handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n324: // It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n325: // It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\n326: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n327: \tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n328: }\n329: \n330: // The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\n331: func debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n332: \tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n333: \tvar startDebounce time.Time\n334: \tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n335: \n336: \tpushCounter := 0\n337: \tdebouncedEvents := 0\n338: \n339: \t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n340: \tvar req *model.PushRequest\n341: \n342: \tfree := true\n343: \tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n344: \n345: \tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n346: \t\tpushFn(req)\n347: \t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n348: \t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n349: \t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n350: \t}\n351: \n352: \tpushWorker := func() {\n353: \t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n354: \t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n355: \t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n356: \t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n357: \t\t\tif req != nil {\n358: \t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n359: \t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n360: \t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n361: \t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n362: \t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n363: \t\t\t\t} else {\n364: \t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n365: \t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n366: \t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n367: \t\t\t\t}\n368: \t\t\t\tfree = false\n369: \t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n370: \t\t\t\treq = nil\n371: \t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n372: \t\t\t}\n373: \t\t} else {\n374: \t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n375: \t\t}\n376: \t}\n377: \n378: \tfor {\n379: \t\tselect {\n380: \t\tcase <-freeCh:\n381: \t\t\tfree = true\n382: \t\t\tpushWorker()\n383: \t\tcase r := <-ch:\n384: \t\t\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\n385: \t\t\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\n386: \t\t\t\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\n387: \t\t\t}\n388: \t\t\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && \t\t\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\n465: \t\t\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\n466: \n467: \t\t\t// Get the next proxy to push. If no change is\n\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n\t// delaying until things settle.\n\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n\n\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n\t// while debouncing. Defaults to 10 seconds. If events keep\n\t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\n\tdebounceMax time.Duration\n\n\t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\n\tenableEDSDebounce bool\n}\n\n// XDSServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\ntype XDSServer struct {\n\t// Env is the model environment.\n\tEnv *model.Environment\n\n\t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\n\t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\n\t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\n\t// different generator for a type.\n\t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\n\tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\n\n\t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators\n\t// may also choose to not send any updates.\n\tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\n\n\t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\n\tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\n\t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n\n\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\n\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\n\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\n\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\n\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\n\n\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\n\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\n\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\n\tpushQueue *PushQueue\n\n\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\n\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\n\n\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\n\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\n\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\n\n\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. Should be same/subset of the CA authenticators.\n\tAuthenticators []security.Authenticator\n\n\tWorkloadEntryController *autoregistration.Controller\n\n\t// serverReady indicates caches have been synced up and server is ready to process requests.\n\tserverReady atomic.Bool\n\n\tDebounceOptions DebounceOptions\n\n\t// Cache for XDS resources\n\tCache model.XdsCache\n\n\t// JwtKeyResolver holds a reference to the JWT key resolver instance.\n\tJwtKeyResolver *model.JwksResolver\n\n\t// ListRemoteClusters collects debug information about other clusters this istiod reads from.\n\tListRemoteClusters func() []cluster.DebugInfo\n\n\t// ClusterAliases are alias names for cluster. When a proxy connects with a cluster ID\n\t// and if it has a different alias we should use that a cluster ID for proxy.\n\tClusterAliases map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID\n\n\t// pushVersion stores the numeric push version. This should be accessed via NextVersion()\n\tpushVersion atomic.Uint64\n\n\t// DiscoveryStartTime is the time since the binary started\n\tDiscoveryStartTime time.Time\n\n\tkrtDebugger *krt.DebugHandler\n}\n\n// NewXDSServer creates XDSServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures\nfunc NewXDSServer(env *model.Environment, clusterAliases map[string]string, debugger *krt.DebugHandler) *XDSServer {\n\tout := &XDSServer{\n\t\tEnv:                 env,\n\t\tGenerators:          map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator{},\n\t\tProxyNeedsPush:      DefaultProxyNeedsPush,\n\t\tconcurrentPushLimit: make(chan struct{}, features.PushThrottle),\n\t\tRequestRateLimit:    rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(features.RequestLimit), 1),\n\t\tInboundUpdates:      atomic.NewInt64(0),\n\t\tCommittedUpdates:    atomic.NewInt64(0),\n\t\tpushChannel:         make(chan *model.PushRequest, 10),\n\t\tpushQueue:           NewPushQueue(),\n\t\tdebugHandlers:       map[string]string{},\n\t\tadsClients:          map[string]*Connection{},\n\t\tkrtDebugger:         debugger,\n\t\tDebounceOptions: DebounceOptions{\n\t\t\tDebounceAfter:     features.DebounceAfter,\n\t\t\tdebounceMax:       features.DebounceMax,\n\t\t\tenableEDSDebounce: features.EnableEDSDebounce,\n\t\t},\n\t\tCache:              env.Cache,\n\t\tDiscoveryStartTime: processStartTime,\n\t}\n\n\tout.ClusterAliases = make(map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID)\n\tfor alias := range clusterAliases {\n\t\tout.ClusterAliases[cluster.ID(alias)] = cluster.ID(clusterAliases[alias])\n\t}\n\n\tout.initJwksResolver()\n\n\treturn out\n}\n\n// initJwkResolver initializes the JWT key resolver to be used.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initJwksResolver() {\n\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\n\t\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n\t}\n\ts.JwtKeyResolver = model.NewJwksResolver(\n\t\tmodel.JwtPubKeyEvictionDuration, model.JwtPubKeyRefreshInterval,\n\t\tmodel.JwtPubKeyRefreshIntervalOnFailure, model.JwtPubKeyRetryInterval)\n\n\t// Flush cached discovery responses when detecting jwt public key change.\n\ts.JwtKeyResolver.PushFunc = func() {\n\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger), Forced: true})\n\t}\n}\n\n// closeJwksResolver shuts down the JWT key resolver used.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) closeJwksResolver() {\n\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\n\t\ts.JwtKeyResolver.Close()\n\t}\n}\n\n// Register adds the ADS handler to the grpc server\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\n\t// Register v3 server\n\tdiscovery.RegisterAggregatedDiscoveryServiceServer(rpcs, s)\n}\n\nvar processStartTime = time.Now()\n\n// CachesSynced is called when caches have been synced so that server can accept connections.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) CachesSynced() {\n\tlog.Infof(\"All caches have been synced up in %v, marking server ready\", time.Since(s.DiscoveryStartTime))\n\ts.serverReady.Store(true)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) IsServerReady() bool {\n\treturn s.serverReady.Load()\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tgo s.WorkloadEntryController.Run(stopCh)\n\tgo s.handleUpdates(stopCh)\n\tgo s.periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh)\n\tgo s.sendPushes(stopCh)\n\tgo s.Cache.Run(stopCh)\n}\n\n// Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\nfunc (s *XDSServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\n\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n\t\t\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\n\t\t\t\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\n\t\t\t\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\n\t\t\t\tout, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n\t\t\t\tif string(out) != \"{}\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push Status: %s\", string(out))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request\nfunc (s *XDSServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\n\t\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\n\t\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n\t\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// Reset the status during the push.\n\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\n\tif oldPushContext != nil {\n\t\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\n\t\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\n\t\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\n\t}\n\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\n\t// saved.\n\tt0 := time.Now()\n\tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\n\tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\n\tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"InitContext %v for push took %s\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\n\tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\n\n\treq.Push = push\n\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n}\n\nfunc nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\n\treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\n}\n\n// Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\n// PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\n// only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n}\n\nvar fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n\n// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n\t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n\t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n\t\t// the cache.\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t}\n\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n\t}\n\ts.pushChannel <- req\n}\n\n// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n}\n\n// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\nfunc debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n\n\tpushCounter := 0\n\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n\n\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n\n\tfree := true\n\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n\n\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tpushWorker := func() {\n\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfree = false\n\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n\t\t\t\treq = nil\n\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeChan = reason1Cnt, idx int\n\t)\n\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n\t\tif idx == 0 {\n\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tidx++\n\t}\n\n\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\n\tcase 0:\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\tcase 1:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\n\tcase 2:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase This method can be safely called concurrently,\n// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\n// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Clients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\n\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Initialization not complete, skip\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tclients = append(clients, con)\n\t}\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\n// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\n\tclients := s.Clients()\n\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\n\t})\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\n// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\nfunc (s *XDSServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\n\t\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\n\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\n\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\treturn s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) NextVersion() string {\n\treturn time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + \"/\" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9717,"cache_read_input_tokens":52872,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":9717},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7a942838-f197-4b2d-b026-89189f05a14f","uuid":"23e45d21-9edc-4c95-b510-68a63fc9cf80"}

DebounceOptions struct {\n\t// DebounceAfter is the delay added to events to wait\n\t// after a registry/config event for debouncing.\n\t// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus\n\t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\n\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n\t// delaying until things settle.\n\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n\n\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n\t// while debouncing. Defaults to 10 seconds. If events keep\n\t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\n\tdebounceMax time.Duration\n\n\t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\n\tenableEDSDebounce bool\n}\n\n// XDSServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\ntype XDSServer struct {\n\t// Env is the model environment.\n\tEnv *model.Environment\n\n\t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\n\t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\n\t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\n\t// different generator for a type.\n\t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\n\tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\n\n\t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators\n\t// may also choose to not send any updates.\n\tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\n\n\t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\n\tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\n\t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n\n\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\n\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\n\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\n\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\n\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\n\n\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\n\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\n\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\n\tpushQueue *PushQueue\n\n\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\n\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\n\n\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\n\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\n\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\n\n\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. atomic.NewInt64(0),\n\t\tCommittedUpdates:    atomic.NewInt64(0),\n\t\tpushChannel:         make(chan *model.PushRequest, 10),\n\t\tpushQueue:           NewPushQueue(),\n\t\tdebugHandlers:       map[string]string{},\n\t\tadsClients:          map[string]*Connection{},\n\t\tkrtDebugger:         debugger,\n\t\tDebounceOptions: DebounceOptions{\n\t\t\tDebounceAfter: server\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\n\t// Register v3 server\n\tdiscovery.RegisterAggregatedDiscoveryServiceServer(rpcs, s)\n}\n\nvar processStartTime = time.Now()\n\n// This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\n// PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\n// only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n}\n\nvar fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n\n// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n\t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n\t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n\t\t// the cache.\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t}\n\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n\t}\n\ts.pushChannel <- req\n}\n\n// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n}\n\n// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\nfunc debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n\n\tpushCounter := 0\n\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n\n\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n\n\tfree := true\n\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n\n\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tpushWorker := func() {\n\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n\t\t\tif req != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfree = false\n\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n\t\t\t\treq = nil\n\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-freeCh:\n\t\t\tfree = true\n\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\tcase r := <-ch:\n\t\t\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\n\t\t\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\n\t\t\t\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\n\t\t\t\tgo func(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t\t\t\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\t\t\t\tupdateSent.Inc()\n\t\t\t\t}(r)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\n\t\t\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\n\t\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\n\t\t\t\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdebouncedEvents++\n\n\t\t\treq = req.Merge(r)\n\t\tcase <-timeChan:\n\t\t\tif free {\n\t\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n\tconfigs := \"\"\n\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tconfigs += key.String()\n\t\tbreak\n\t}\n\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\n\t\tmore := \" and \" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \" more configs\"\n\t\tconfigs += more\n\t}\n\treturn configs\n}\n\nfunc reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n\tvar (\n\t\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\n\t\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\n\t)\n\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n\t\tif idx == 0 {\n\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tidx++\n\t}\n\n\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\n\tcase 0:\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\tcase 1:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\n\tcase 2:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\n\t\t\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\n\t\t\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\n\n\t\t\t// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.\n\t\t\tclient, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()\n\t\t\tif shuttingdown {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trecordPushTriggers(push.Reason)\n\t\t\t// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.\n\t\t\tdoneFunc := func() {\n\t\t\t\tqueue.MarkDone(client)\n\t\t\t\t<-semaphore\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tproxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())\n\t\t\tvar closed <-chan struct{}\n\t\t\tif client.deltaStream != nil {\n\t\t\t\tclosed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tclosed = client.StreamDone()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\t\tpushEv := &Event{\n\t\t\t\t\tpushRequest: push,\n\t\t\t\t\tdone:        doneFunc,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\tcase client.PushCh() <- pushEv:\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\tcase <-closed: // grpc stream was closed\n\t\t\t\t\tdoneFunc()\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Client closed connection %v\", client.ID())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this\n// method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\n// if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\n// reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\n\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\n\tpush.PushVersion = version\n\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\n\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\n\n\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\n\n\treturn push\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\n}\n\n// Shutdown shuts down XDSServer components.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Shutdown() {\n\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\n}\n\n// Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\n// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\n// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Clients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\n\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Initialization not complete, skip\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tclients = append(clients, con)\n\t}\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\n// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\n\tclients := s.Clients()\n\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\n\t})\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\n// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\nfunc (s *XDSServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\n\t\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\n\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\n\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\treturn s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) NextVersion() string {\n\treturn time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + \"/\" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
