This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n140: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n141: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \\\", stype,\\n142: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\\n143: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n144: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\\n145: \\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\\n149: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n150: \\t\\treturn s.pushXds(con,\\n151: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\\n156: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n161: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n162: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n163: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n166: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n167: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n168: \\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n169: \\t\\tDelta:  delta,\\n170: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\\n174: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n175: \\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\\n176: \\t// computation.\\n177: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n178: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n185: \\treturn s.Stream(stream)\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n189: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n190: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n191: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n192: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n193: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n194: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n195: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n198: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n199: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n200: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n201: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n202: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n203: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n204: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n205: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n206: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\n209: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n210: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n211: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n212: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\n215: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n217: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n221: \\tif err != nil {\\n222: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\tif ids != nil {\\n225: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\\\", peerAddr)\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n231: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n232: \\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n233: \\tcon.ids = ids\\n234: \\tcon.s = s\\n235: \\treturn xds.Stream(con)\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\\n239: // to the tracking map.\\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n241: \\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\\n242: \\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\\n247: \\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\\n248: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\\n251: \\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\\n252: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\\n253: \\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\\n254: \\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\\n255: \\tcon.node = node\\n256: \\tcon.proxy = proxy\\n257: \\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\\n258: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\\\")\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\t// Authorize xds clients\\n262: \\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\treturn err\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\\n267: \\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\\n268: \\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\\n269: \\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\\n270: \\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\\n271: \\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\\n272: \\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\\n273: \\t// proxy\\n274: \\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\\n275: \\n276: \\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\\n277: \\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\\n278: \\t\\ts.closeConnection(con)\\n279: \\t\\treturn err\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\\n285: \\tif con.ID() == \\\"\\\" {\\n286: \\t\\treturn\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\\n289: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\\n293: \\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\\n294: \\treturn node + \\\"-\\\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\\n301: \\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\\n302: \\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\\n310: \\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\\n311: \\tproxy.XdsNode = node\\n312: \\treturn proxy, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\\n318: \\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\\n319: \\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\\n320: \\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\\n321: \\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\\n322: \\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\\n323: \\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\\n325: \\t\\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\\n326: \\t\\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\\n327: \\t\\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\\n328: \\t\\t\\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\\n329: \\t\\t\\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\\n330: \\t\\t\\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\\n331: \\t\\t}\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\\n334: \\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\\n335: \\t\\tproxy.Labels,\\n336: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n337: \\t\\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\\n338: \\t\\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\\n339: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\\n340: \\t)\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\\n344: \\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\\n345: \\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\\n346: \\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\\n347: \\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\\n348: \\t\\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\\n349: \\t\\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\\n350: \\t\\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\\n351: \\t\\tif localityLabel != \\\"\\\" {\\n352: \\t\\t\\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\\n353: \\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\treturn &core.Locality{\\n357: \\t\\tRegion:  region,\\n358: \\t\\tZone:    zone,\\n359: \\t\\tSubZone: subzone,\\n360: \\t}\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\\n366: \\tproxy := con.proxy\\n367: \\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\\n368: \\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\\n369: \\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\\n370: \\t\\treturn err\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\\n373: \\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\\n374: \\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\\n375: \\n376: \\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\\n377: \\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\\n378: \\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \\\"\\\" {\\n379: \\t\\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\treturn nil\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\\n386: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n387: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n388: \\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\\n389: \\t// 1. shouldResetGateway {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\t\\t}\\n433: \\t}\\n434: \\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\\n435: \\tif \\t}\\n446: }\\n447: \\n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n44: )\\n45: \\n46: var (\\n47: \\tlog = xds.Log\\n48: \\n49: \\t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\\n50: \\tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\\n51: )\\n52: \\n53: // Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\\n54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\\n55: \\n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\\\"K_REVISION\\\", \\\"\\\",\\n57: \\t\\\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\\\").Get()\\n58: \\n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\\n61: \\n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\\n63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\\n64: \\n65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\\n66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\\n67: \\n68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\\n69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\\n70: \\n71: type Connection struct {\\n72: \\txds.Connection\\n73: \\n74: \\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\\n75: \\t// This is included in internal events.\\n76: \\tnode *core.Node\\n77: \\n78: \\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\\n79: \\tproxy *model.Proxy\\n80: \\n81: \\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\\n82: \\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\\n83: \\n84: \\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\\n85: \\n86: \\ts   *DiscoveryServer\\n87: \\tids []string\\n88: }\\n89: \\n90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\\n91: \\treturn &conn.Connection\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\\n95: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n96: }\\n97: \\n98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\\n99: type Event struct {\\n100: \\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\\n101: \\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\\n102: \\n103: \\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\\n104: \\tdone func()\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\\n108: \\treturn &Connection{\\n109: \\t\\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\\n110: \\t}\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\\n114: \\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\\n118: \\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\\n122: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\\n126: \\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\\n130: \\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\\n131: \\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\\n132: \\tpushEv.done()\\n133: \\treturn err\\n134: }\\n135: \\n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n140: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n141: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \\\", stype,\\n142: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\\n143: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n144: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\\n145: \\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\\n149: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n150: \\t\\treturn s.pushXds(con,\\n151: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\\n156: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n161: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n162: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n163: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n166: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n167: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n168: \\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n169: \\t\\tDelta:  delta,\\n170: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\\n174: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n175: \\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\\n176: \\t// computation.\\n177: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n178: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n185: \\treturn s.Stream(stream)\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n189: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n190: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n191: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n192: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n193: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n194: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n195: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n198: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n199: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n200: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n201: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n202: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n203: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n204: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n205: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n206: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\n209: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n210: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n211: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n212: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\n215: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n217: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n221: \\tif err != nil {\\n222: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\tif ids != nil {\\n225: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\\\", peerAddr)\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n231: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n232: \\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n233: \\tcon.ids = ids\\n234: \\tcon.s = s\\n235: \\treturn xds.Stream(con)\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\\n239: // to the tracking map.\\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n241: \\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\\n242: \\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\\n247: \\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\\n248: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\\n251: \\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\\n252: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\\n253: \\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\\n254: \\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\\n255: \\tcon.node = node\\n256: \\tcon.proxy = proxy\\n257: \\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\\n258: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\\\")\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\t// Authorize xds clients\\n262: \\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\treturn err\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\\n267: \\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\\n268: \\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\\n269: \\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\\n270: \\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\\n271: \\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\\n272: \\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\\n273: \\t// proxy\\n274: \\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\\n275: \\n276: \\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\\n277: \\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\\n278: \\t\\ts.closeConnection(con)\\n279: \\t\\treturn err\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\\n285: \\tif con.ID() == \\\"\\\" {\\n286: \\t\\treturn\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\\n289: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\\n293: \\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\\n294: \\treturn node + \\\"-\\\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\\n301: \\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\\n302: \\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\\n310: \\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\\n311: \\tproxy.XdsNode = node\\n312: \\treturn proxy, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\\n318: \\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\\n319: \\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\\n320: \\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\\n321: \\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\\n322: \\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\\n323: \\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\\n325: \\t\\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\\n326: \\t\\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\\n327: \\t\\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\\n328: \\t\\t\\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\\n329: \\t\\t\\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\\n330: \\t\\t\\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\\n331: \\t\\t}\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\\n334: \\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\\n335: \\t\\tproxy.Labels,\\n336: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n337: \\t\\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\\n338: \\t\\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\\n339: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\\n340: \\t)\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\\n344: \\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\\n345: \\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\\n346: \\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\\n347: \\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\\n348: \\t\\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\\n349: \\t\\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\\n350: \\t\\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\\n351: \\t\\tif localityLabel != \\\"\\\" {\\n352: \\t\\t\\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\\n353: \\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\treturn &core.Locality{\\n357: \\t\\tRegion:  region,\\n358: \\t\\tZone:    zone,\\n359: \\t\\tSubZone: subzone,\\n360: \\t}\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\\n366: \\tproxy := con.proxy\\n367: \\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\\n368: \\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\\n369: \\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\\n370: \\t\\treturn err\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\\n373: \\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\\n374: \\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\\n375: \\n376: \\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\\n377: \\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\\n378: \\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \\\"\\\" {\\n379: \\t\\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\treturn nil\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\\n386: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n387: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n388: \\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\\n389: \\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\\n390: \\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\\n391: \\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\\n392: \\t\\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\\n393: \\t\\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\\n394: \\t\\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\\n395: \\t\\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\\n396: \\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\t// only recompute workload labels when\\n400: \\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\\n401: \\t// 2. proxy update\\n402: \\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\\n403: \\tif recomputeLabels {\\n404: \\t\\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\\n405: \\t\\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\\n406: \\t}\\n407: \\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\\n408: \\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\\n409: \\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\\n410: \\t// applicable to this proxy\\n411: \\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\\n412: \\tif request == nil {\\n413: \\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n414: \\t} else {\\n415: \\t\\tpush = request.Push\\n416: \\t\\tif request.Forced {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\\n420: \\t\\t\\tswitch conf.Kind {\\n421: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\\n422: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n423: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Gateway:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n425: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Ingress:\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\t\\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\t\\t}\\n433: \\t}\\n434: \\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\\n435: \\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\\n436: \\t\\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\\n437: \\t}\\n438: \\t// only compute gateways for \\\"router\\\" type proxy.\\n439: \\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\\n440: \\t\\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\\n443: \\tif request != nil {\\n444: \\t\\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\\n445: \\t}\\n446: }\\n447: \\n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\\n450: \\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\\n451: \\t\\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\\n452: \\t\\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\\n453: \\t\\tif !event.Healthy {\\n454: \\t\\t\\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\\n457: \\t}\\n458: }\\n459: \\n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\\n462: // This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\\n463: // pattern.\\n464: //\\n465: // The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\\n466: // list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\\n467: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\\n468: \\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\\n472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\\n473: \\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\\n474: \\n475: \\tif pushRequest.Full {\\n476: \\t\\t// Update Proxy with current information.\\n477: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n478: \\t}\\n479: \\n480: \\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n481: \\tif !needsPush {\\n482: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\\\", con.ID())\\n483: \\t\\treturn nil\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\t// Send pushes to all generators\\n487: \\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\\n488: \\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\\n489: \\tfor _, w := range wrl {\\n490: \\t\\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\t}\\n494: \\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\\n495: \\treturn nil\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: // PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\\n499: // order after the types listed here\\n500: var PushOrder = []string{\\n501: \\tv3.ClusterType,\\n502: \\tv3.EndpointType,\\n503: \\tv3.ListenerType,\\n504: \\tv3.RouteType,\\n505: \\tv3.SecretType,\\n506: \\tv3.AddressType,\\n507: \\tv3.WorkloadType,\\n508: \\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\\n509: }\\n510: \\n511: // KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\\n512: var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\\n513: \\n514: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\\n515: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n516: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n517: \\treturn len(s.adsClients)\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\\n521: \\tvar connection *Connection\\n522: \\n523: \\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\\n524: \\t\\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\\n525: \\t\\t\\tconnection = v\\n526: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n527: \\t\\t}\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\n530: \\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\\n531: \\tif connection == nil {\\n532: \\t\\treturn\\n533: \\t}\\n534: \\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n535: \\t\\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\\n536: \\t\\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\\n537: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\\\", currentlyPending)\\n538: \\t\\t}\\n539: \\t}\\n540: \\n541: \\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\\n542: knative\").Get()\n58: \n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n61: \n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n104: \tdone func()\n105: }\n106: \n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n108: \treturn &Connection{\n109: \t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n110: \t}\n111: }\n112: \n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n114: \treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n115: }\n116: \n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\n118: \tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n119: }\n120: \n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n122: \treturn conn.proxy\n123: }\n124: \n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n126: \treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n127: }\n128: \n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n130: \tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n131: \terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n132: \tpushEv.done()\n133: \treturn err\n134: }\n135: \n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n140: \tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n141: \tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n142: \t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n143: \tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n144: \t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n145: \t\treturn nil\n146: \t}\n147: \n148: \t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n149: \tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n150: \t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n151: \t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n152: \t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n153: \t}\n154: \n155: \tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n156: \tif !shouldRespond {\n157: \t\treturn nil\n158: \t}\n159: \n160: \trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n161: \t\tFull:   true,\n162: \t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n163: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n164: \n165: \t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n166: \t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n167: \t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n168: \t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n169: \t\tDelta:  delta,\n170: \t\tForced: true,\n171: \t}\n172: \n173: \t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n174: \t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n175: \t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n176: \t// computation.\n177: \tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n178: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n179: \t}\n180: \treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n181: }\n182: \n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n185: \treturn s.Stream(stream)\n186: }\n187: \n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n189: \tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n190: \t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n191: \t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n192: \t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n193: \t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n194: \t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n195: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n196: \t}\n197: \t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n198: \t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n199: \t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n200: \t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n201: \t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n202: \t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n203: \t// ip tables update latencies.\n204: \t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n205: \tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n206: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n207: \t}\n208: \n209: \tctx := stream.Context()\n210: \tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n211: \tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n212: \t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n213: \t}\n214: \n215: \tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n216: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n217: \t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n218: \t}\n219: \n220: \tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n221: \tif err != nil {\n222: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n223: \t}\n224: \tif ids != nil {\n225: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n226: \t} else {\n227: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n228: \t}\n229: \n230: \t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n231: \ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n232: \tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n233: \tcon.ids = ids\n234: \tcon.s = s\n235: \treturn xds.Stream(con)\n236: }\n237: \n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n239: // to the tracking map.\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n241: \t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n242: \tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n243: \tif err != nil {\n244: \t\treturn err\n245: \t}\n246: \t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n247: \tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n248: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n249: \t}\n250: \t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n251: \t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n252: \tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n253: \t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n254: \tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n255: \tcon.node = node\n256: \tcon.proxy = proxy\n257: \tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n258: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n259: \t}\n260: \n261: \t// Authorize xds clients\n262: \tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n263: \t\treturn err\n264: \t}\n265: \n266: \t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n267: \t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n268: \t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n269: \t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n270: \t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n271: \ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n272: \t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n273: \t// proxy\n274: \tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n275: \n276: \t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n277: \tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n278: \t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n279: \t\treturn err\n280: \t}\n281: \treturn nil\n282: }\n283: \n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n285: \tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n286: \t\treturn\n287: \t}\n288: \ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n289: \ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n290: }\n291: \n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\n293: \tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n294: \treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n295: }\n296: \n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n301: \tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n302: \tif err != nil {\n303: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n304: \t}\n305: \tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n306: \tif err != nil {\n307: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n308: \t}\n309: \t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n310: \tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n311: \tproxy.XdsNode = node\n312: \treturn proxy, nil\n313: }\n314: \n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n318: \t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n319: \t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n320: \t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n321: \t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n322: \tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n323: \tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n324: \t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n325: \t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n326: \t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n327: \t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n328: \t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n329: \t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n330: \t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n331: \t\t}\n332: \t}\n333: \t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n334: \tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n335: \t\tproxy.Labels,\n336: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n337: \t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n338: \t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n339: \t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n340: \t)\n341: }\n342: \n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n344: \tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n345: \tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n346: \tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n347: \tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n348: \t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n349: \t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n350: \t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n351: \t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n352: \t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n353: \t\t}\n354: \t\treturn nil\n355: \t}\n356: \treturn &core.Locality{\n357: \t\tRegion:  region,\n358: \t\tZone:    zone,\n359: \t\tSubZone: subzone,\n360: \t}\n361: }\n362: \n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n366: \tproxy := con.proxy\n367: \t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n368: \t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n369: \tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n370: \t\treturn err\n371: \t}\n372: \ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n373: \t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n374: \tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n375: \n376: \tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n377: \t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n378: \tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n379: \t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n380: \t}\n381: \n382: \treturn nil\n383: }\n384: \n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n386: \tproxy.Lock()\n387: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n388: \tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n389: \t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n390: \t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n391: \tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n392: \t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n393: \t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n394: \t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n395: \t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n396: \t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n397: \t}\n398: \n399: \t// only recompute workload labels when\n400: \t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n401: \t// 2. proxy update\n402: \trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n403: \tif recomputeLabels {\n404: \t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n405: \t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n406: \t}\n407: \t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n408: \t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n409: \t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n410: \t// applicable to this proxy\n411: \tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n412: \tif request == nil {\n413: \t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n414: \t} else {\n415: \t\tpush = request.Push\n416: \t\tif request.Forced {\n417: \t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n420: \t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n421: \t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n422: \t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n423: \t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n424: \t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n425: \t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n426: \t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n427: \t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n428: \t\t\t}\n429: \t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n430: \t\t\t\tbreak\n431: \t\t\t}\n432: \t\t}\n433: \t}\n434: \t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n435: \tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n436: \t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n437: \t}\n438: \t// only pending\", currentlyPending)\n588: \t\t}\n589: \t}\n590: \treq.Start = time.Now()\n591: \tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n592: \t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n593: \t}\n594: }\n595: \n596: func to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\\n247: \\t\\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\\n248: \\t\\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\\n249: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n250: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n251: \\t\\t}\\n252: \\t\\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\\n253: \\t\\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\\n254: \\t\\tvar ids []string\\n255: \\t\\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\\n256: \\t\\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\\n258: \\t\\t\\t// If one authenticator passes, append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Authenticator %s: %v\\\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\\n264: \\t\\t}\\n265: \\t\\tif ids == nil {\\n266: \\t\\t\\tistiolog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\\\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \\\"authentication failure).\\n268: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\\n269: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n270: \\t\\t}\\n271: \\t\\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\\n272: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\\n274: \\t\\t\\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\\\", ids, req.URL.Path)\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n278: \\t\\t\\t}\\n279: \\t\\t\\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\\n280: \\t\\t\\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\\n281: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\\n282: \\t\\t} else {\\n283: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t}\\n286: }\\n287: \\n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\\n289: \\tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\\n290: \\tif err != nil {\\n291: \\t\\treturn false\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\n294: \\tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\\n295: \\treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\\n300: \\tfor _, id := range identities \\\"\\\"\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\\n313: \\treturn nil\\n656: }\\n657: \\n658: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn *Connection, req *model.PushRequest, ts []string) map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource {\\n659: \\tdumps addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\n233: \tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\n234: ) {\n235: \ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\n236: \t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\n237: \tif internalMux != nil {\n238: \t\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\n239: \t}\n240: \t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\n241: \tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\n242: }\n243: \n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\n245: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n246: \t\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\n247: \t\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\n248: \t\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\n249: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n250: \t\t\treturn\n251: \t\t}\n252: \t\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\n253: \t\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\n254: \t\tvar ids []string\n255: \t\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\n256: \t\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\n257: \t\t\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\n258: \t\t\t// If one authenticator passes, return\n259: \t\t\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\n260: \t\t\t\tids = u.Identities\n261: \t\t\t\tbreak\n262: \t\t\t}\n263: \t\t\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\"Authenticator %s: %v\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\n264: \t\t}\n265: \t\tif ids == nil {\n266: \t\t\tistiolog.Errorf(\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\n267: \t\t\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \"authentication failure).\n268: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\n269: \t\t\treturn\n270: \t\t}\n271: \t\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\n272: \t\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\n273: \t\t\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\n274: \t\t\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\n275: \t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\", ids, req.URL.Path)\n276: \t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\n277: \t\t\t\treturn\n278: \t\t\t}\n279: \t\t\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\n280: \t\t\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\n281: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\n282: \t\t} else {\n283: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n284: \t\t}\n285: \t}\n286: }\n287: \n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\n289: \tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\n290: \tif err != nil {\n291: \t\treturn false\n292: \t}\n293: \n294: \tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\n295: \treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\n296: }\n297: \n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\n300: \tfor _, id := range identities {\n301: \t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(id)\n302: \t\tif err != nil {\n303: \t\t\tcontinue\n304: \t\t}\n305: \t\treturn spiffeID.Namespace\n306: \t}\n307: \treturn \"\"\n308: }\n309: \n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\n313: \tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(identities)\n314: \n315: \t// deny if no valid identity found\n316: \tif namespace == \"\" {\n317: \t\treturn false\n318: \t}\n319: \n320: \t// get system namespace (istio-system by default, or mesh root namespace)\n321: \tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\n322: \tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \"\" {\n323: \t\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\n324: \t}\n325: \n326: \t// allow all if identity is from system namespace\n327: \tif namespace == systemNamespace {\n328: \t\treturn true\n329: \t}\n330: \n331: \t// non-system namespace: only allow specific endpoints\n332: \tdebugPath := strings.TrimPrefix(req.URL.Path, \"/debug/\")\n333: \t_, allowed := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugPath]\n334: \treturn allowed\n335: }\n336: \n337: // Syncz dumps the synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\n338: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Syncz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n339: \tnamespace := req.URL.Query().Get(\"namespace\")\n340: \n341: \tsyncz := make([]SyncStatus, 0)\n342: \tfor _, con := range s.SortedClients() {\n343: \t\tnode := con.proxy\n344: \t\tif node != nil && (namespace == \"\" || node.GetNamespace() == namespace) {\n345: \t\t\twrs := node.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\n346: \t\t\tres := make(map[string]ResourceStatus, len(wrs))\n347: \t\t\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\n348: \t\t\t\tres[wr.TypeUrl] = ResourceStatus{\n349: \t\t\t\t\tSent:      wr.NonceSent,\n350: \t\t\t\t\tAcked:     wr.NonceAcked,\n351: \t\t\t\t\tSentTime:  wr.LastSendTime,\n352: \t\t\t\t\tLastError: wr.LastError,\n353: \t\t\t\t}\n354: \t\t\t}\n355: \t\t\tsyncz = append(syncz, SyncStatus{\n356: \t\t\t\tProxyID:              node.ID,\n357: \t\t\t\tProxyType:            node.Type,\n358: \t\t\t\tClusterID:            node.GetClusterID().String(),\n359: \t\t\t\tIstioVersion:         node.GetIstioVersion(),\n360: \t\t\t\tClusterSent:          node.NonceSent(v3.ClusterType),\n361: \t\t\t\tClusterAcked:         node.NonceAcked(v3.ClusterType),\n362: \t\t\t\tListenerSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.ListenerType),\n363: \t\t\t\tListenerAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.ListenerType),\n364: \t\t\t\tRouteSent:            node.NonceSent(v3.RouteType),\n365: \t\t\t\tRouteAcked:           node.NonceAcked(v3.RouteType),\n366: \t\t\t\tEndpointSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.EndpointType),\n367: \t\t\t\tEndpointAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.EndpointType),\n368: \t\t\t\tExtensionConfigSent:  node.NonceSent(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\n369: \t\t\t\tExtensionConfigAcked: node.NonceAcked(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\n370: \t\t\t\tResources:            res,\n371: \t\t\t})\n372: \t\t}\n373: \t}\n374: \twriteJSON(w, syncz, req)\n375: }\n376: \n377: // registryz providees debug support for registry - adding and listing model items.\n378: // Can be combined with the push debug interface to reproduce changes.\n379: func (s *DiscoveryServer) registryz(w {\n392: \tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n393: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n394: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Failed to parse request\\n\"))\n395: \t\treturn\n396: \t}\n397: \tif req.Form.Get(\"clear\") != \"\" {\n398: \t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n399: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Cache cleared\\n\"))\n400: \t\treturn\n401: \t}\n402: \tif req.Form.Get(\"sizes\") != \"\" {\n403: \t\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\n404: \t\traw := make(map[string]int, len(snapshot))\n405: \t\ttotalSize := 0\n406: \t\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\n407: \t\t\tif resource == nil {\n408: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n409: \t\t\t}\n410: \t\t\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\n411: \t\t\tsz := len(resource.Resource.GetValue())\n412: \t\t\traw[resourceType] += sz\n413: \t\t\ttotalSize += sz\n414: \t\t}\n415: \t\tres := make(map[string]string, len(raw))\n416: \t\tfor k, v := range raw {\n417: \t\t\tres[k] = util.ByteCount(v)\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tres[\"total\"] = util.ByteCount(totalSize)\n420: \t\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\n421: \t\treturn\n422: \t}\n423: \tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\n424: \tresources := make(map[string][]string, len(snapshot)) // Key is typeUrl and value is resource names.\n425: \tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\n426: \t\tif resource == nil {\n427: \t\t\tcontinue\n428: \t\t}\n429: \t\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\n430: \t\tresources[resourceType] = append(resources[resourceType], resource.Name)\n431: \t}\n432: \twriteJSON(w, resources, req)\n433: }\n434: \n435: // kubernetesConfig wraps a config.Config with a custom marshaling method that matches a Kubernetes\n436: // object structure.\n437: type kubernetesConfig struct {\n438: \tconfig.Config\n439: }\n440: \n441: func (k kubernetesConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n442: \tcfg, err := crd.ConvertConfig(k.Config)\n443: \tif err != nil {\n444: \t\treturn nil, err\n445: \t}\n446: \treturn json.Marshal(cfg)\n447: }\n448: \n449: // Config debugging.\n450: func (s *DiscoveryServer) configz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n451: \tconfigs := make([]kubernetesConfig, 0)\n452: \tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.ConfigStore == nil {\n453: \t\treturn\n454: \t}\n455: \ts.Env.ConfigStore.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\n456: \t\tcfg := s.Env.ConfigStore.List(schema.GroupVersionKind(), \"\")\n457: \t\tfor _, c := range cfg {\n458: \t\t\tconfigs = append(configs, kubernetesConfig{c})\n459: \t\t}\n460: \t\treturn false\n461: \t})\n462: \twriteJSON(w, configs, req)\n463: }\n464: \n465: // SidecarScope debugging\n466: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sidecarz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n467: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n468: \tif con == nil {\n469: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n470: \t\treturn\n471: \t}\n472: \twriteJSON(w, con.proxy.SidecarScope, req)\n473: }\n474: \n475: // Resource debugging.\n476: func (s *DiscoveryServer) resourcez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n477: \tschemas := make([]config.GroupVersionKind, 0)\n478: \n479: \tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.ConfigStore != nil {\n480: \n507: func (s *DiscoveryServer) telemetryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n508: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n509: \tif proxyID != \"\" && con == nil {\n510: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n511: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n512: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n513: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n514: \t\treturn\n515: \t}\n516: \tif con == nil {\n517: \t\tinfo := TelemetryDebug{\n518: \t\t\tTelemetries: s.globalPushContext().Telemetry,\n519: \t\t}\n520: \t\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\n521: \t\treturn\n522: \t}\n523: \twriteJSON(w, s.globalPushContext().Telemetry.Debug(con.proxy), req)\n524: }\n525: \n526: // connectionsHandler implements interface for displaying current connections.\n527: // It is mapped to /debug/connections.\n528: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionsHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n529: \tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\n530: \tconnections := s.SortedClients()\n531: \tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\n532: \n533: \tfor _, c := range connections {\n534: \t\tadsClient := AdsClient{\n535: \t\t\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\n536: \t\t\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\n537: \t\t\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\n538: \t\t}\n539: \t\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\n540: \t}\n541: \twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\n542: }\n543: \n544: // adsz implements a status and debug interface for ADS.\n545: // It is mapped to /debug/adsz\n546: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n547: \tif s.handlePushRequest(w, req) {\n548: \t\treturn\n549: \t}\n550: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n551: \tif proxyID != \"\" && con == nil {\n552: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n553: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n554: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n555: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. adsClient)\n583: \t}\n584: \twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\n585: }\n586: \n587: func (s *DiscoveryServer) typedConfigDumpHandler(typ string) http.HandlerFunc {\n588: \treturn func(w // ConfigDump returns information in the form of the Envoy admin API config dump for the specified proxy\n597: // The dump will only contain dynamic listeners/clusters/routes and can be used to compare what an Envoy instance\n598: // should look like according to Pilot vs what it currently does look like.\n599: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigDump(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n600: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n601: \tif con == nil {\n602: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n603: \t\treturn\n604: \t}\n605: \tif ts := s.getResourceTypes(req); len(ts) != 0 {\n606: \t\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, ts)\n607: \t\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\n608: \t\tfor _, resource := range resources {\n609: \t\t\tfor _, rr := range resource {\n610: \t\t\t\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\n611: \t\t\t}\n612: \t\t}\n613: \t\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\n614: \t\treturn\n615: \t}\n616: \n617: \tif con.proxy.IsZTunnel() {\n618: \t\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, []string{v3.AddressType})\n619: \t\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\n620: \t\tfor _, resource := range resources {\n621: \t\t\tfor _, rr := range resource {\n622: \t\t\t\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\n623: \t\t\t}\n624: \t\t}\n625: \t\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\n626: \t\treturn\n627: \t}\n628: \n629: \tincludeEds := req.URL.Query().Has(\"include_eds\")\n630: \tdump, err := s.connectionConfigDump(con, includeEds)\n631: \tif err != nil {\n632: \t\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\n633: \t\treturn\n634: \t}\n635: \twriteJSON(w, dump, req)\n636: }\n637: \n638: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getResourceTypes(req *http.Request) []string {\n639: \tif shortTypes := req.URL.Query().Get(\"types\"); shortTypes != \"\" {\n640: \t\tts := strings.Split(shortTypes, \",\")\n641: \n642: \t\tresourceTypes := sets.New[string]()\n643: \t\tfor _, t := range ts {\n644: \t\t\trType := v3.GetResourceType(t)\n645: \t\t\tresourceTypes.Insert(rType)\n646: \t\t\t// special case for AddressType, include WorkloadType as well\n647: \t\t\t// because they shared same short type name `WDS`\n648: \t\t\tif rType == v3.AddressType {\n649: \t\t\t\tresourceTypes.Insert(v3.WorkloadType)\n650: \t\t\t}\n651: \t\t}\n652: \n653: \t\treturn resourceTypes.UnsortedList()\n654: \t}\n655: \treturn nil\n656: }\n657: \n658: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn *Connection, req *model.PushRequest, ts []string) map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource {\n659: \tdumps := make(map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource)\n660: \tif req == nil {\n661: \t\treq = &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\n662: \t}\n663: \n664: \tfor _, resourceType := range ts {\n665: \t\tw := conn.proxy.GetWatchedResource(resourceType)\n666: \t\tif w == nil {\n667: \t\t\t// Not watched, skip\n668: \t\t\tcontinue\n669: \t\t}\n670: \t\tgen := s.findGenerator(resourceType, conn)\n671: \t\tif gen == nil {\n672: \t\t\t// No generator found, skip\n673: \t\t\tcontinue\n674: \t\t}\n675: \t\tif resource, _, err := gen.Generate(conn.proxy, w, req); err == nil {\n676: \t\t\tfor _, rr := range resource {\n677: \t\t\t\tswitch resourceType {\n678: \t\t\t\tcase v3.SecretType:\n679: \t\t\t\t\t// Secrets must be redacted\n680: \t\t\t\t\tsecret := &tls.Secret{}\n681: \t\t\t\t\tif err := rr.Resource.UnmarshalTo(secret); err != nil {\n682: \t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal secret: %v\", err)\n683: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n684: \t\t\t\t\t}\n685: \t\t\t\t\tif secret.GetTlsCertificate() != nil {\n686: \t\t\t\t\t\t// When utilizing XDS caching, the resource object is shared, so modifying this would modify the cached item\n687: \t\t\t\t\t\t// Make a clone\n688: \t\t\t\t\t\trr = protomarshal.Clone(rr)\n689: \t\t\t\t\t\tsecret.GetTlsCertificate().PrivateKey = &core.DataSource{\n690: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tSpecifier: &core.DataSource_InlineBytes{\n691: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tInlineBytes: []byte(\"[redacted]\"),\n692: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n693: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n694: \t\t\t\t\t}\n695: \t\t\t\t\trr.Resource = protoconv.MessageToAny(secret)\n696: \t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n697: \t\t\t\tcase v3.ExtensionConfigurationType:\n698: \t\t\t\t\ttce := &core.TypedExtensionConfig{}\n699: \t\t\t\t\tif err := rr.GetResource().UnmarshalTo(tce); err != nil {\n700: \t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal extension: %v\", err)\n701: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n702: \t\t\t\t\t}\n703: \n704: \t\t\t\t\tswitch tce.TypedConfig.TypeUrl {\n705: \t\t\t\t\tcase xds.WasmHTTPFilterType:\n706: \t\t\t\t\t\tw := &wasm.Wasm{}\n707: \t\t\t\t\t\tif err := tce.TypedConfig.UnmarshalTo(w); err != nil {\n708: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal wasm filter: %v\", err)\n709: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n710: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n711: \t\t\t\t\t\t// Redact Wasm secret env variable.\n712: \t\t\t\t\t\tvmenvs := w.GetConfig().GetVmConfig().EnvironmentVariables\n713: \t\t\t\t\t\tif vmenvs != nil {\n714: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tif _, found := vmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv]; found {\n715: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tvmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv] = \"<Redacted>\"\n716: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n717: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n718: \t\t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], &discoveryv3.Resource{\n719: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tName:     w.Config.Name,\n720: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(w),\n721: \t\t\t\t\t\t})\n722: \t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n723: \t\t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n724: \t\t\t\t\t}\n725: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n726: \t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n727: \t\t\t\t}\n728: \t\t\t}\n729: \t\t} else {\n730: \t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"generate failed for request resource type (%v): %v\", resourceType, err)\n731: \t\t\tcontinue\n732: \t\t}\n733: \t}\n734: \n735: \treturn dumps\n736: }\n737: \n738: // connectionConfigDump converts the connection internal state into an Envoy Admin API config dump proto\n739: // It is used in debugging to create a consistent object for comparison between Envoy and Pilot outputs\n740: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionConfigDump(conn *Connection, includeEds bool) lists all the supported debug endpoints.\n936: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Debug(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n937: \ttype debugEndpoint struct {\n938: \t\tName string\n939: \t\tHref // list all the supported debug commands in json.\n962: func (s *DiscoveryServer) list(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n963: \tvar cmdNames []string\n964: \tfor k := range s.debugHandlers {\n965: \t\tkey := strings.Replace(k, \"/debug/\", \"\", -1)\n966: \t\t// exclude current list command\n967: \t\tif key == \"list\" {\n968: \t\t\tcontinue\n969: \t\t}\n970: \t\t// can not support *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\\n233: \\tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\\n234: ) {\\n235: \\ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\\n236: \\t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\\n237: \\tif internalMux != nil {\\n238: \\t\\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\\n239: \\t}\\n240: \\t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\\n241: \\tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\\n242: }\\n243: \\n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\\n245: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n246: \\t\\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\\n247: \\t\\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\\n248: \\t\\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\\n249: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n250: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n251: \\t\\t}\\n252: \\t\\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\\n253: \\t\\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\\n254: \\t\\tvar ids []string\\n255: \\t\\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\\n256: \\t\\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\\n258: \\t\\t\\t// If one authenticator passes, return\\n259: \\t\\t\\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\tids = u.Identities\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n262: \\t\\t\\t}\\n263: \\t\\t\\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Authenticator %s: %v\\\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\\n264: \\t\\t}\\n265: \\t\\tif ids == nil {\\n266: \\t\\t\\tistiolog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\\\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \\\"authentication failure).\\n268: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\\n269: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n270: \\t\\t}\\n271: \\t\\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\\n272: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\\n274: \\t\\t\\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\\\", ids, req.URL.Path)\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n278: \\t\\t\\t}\\n279: \\t\\t\\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\\n280: \\t\\t\\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\\n281: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\\n282: \\t\\t} else {\\n283: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t}\\n286: }\\n287: \\n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\\n289: \\tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\\n290: \\tif err != nil {\\n291: \\t\\treturn false\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\n294: \\tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\\n295: \\treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\\n300: \\tfor _, id := range identities {\\n301: \\t\\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(id)\\n302: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n304: \\t\\t}\\n305: \\t\\treturn spiffeID.Namespace\\n306: \\t}\\n307: \\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\\n313: \\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(identities)\\n314: \\n315: \\t// deny if no valid identity found\\n316: \\tif namespace == \\\"\\\" {\\n317: \\t\\treturn false\\n318: \\t}\\n319: \\n320: \\t// get of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\\n338: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Syncz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n339: \\tnamespace := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"namespace\\\")\\n340: \\treturn nil\\n656: }\\n657: \\n658: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn *Connection, req *model.PushRequest, ts []string) map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource {\\n659: \\tdumps If no change is\n    51→\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n    52→\t// delaying until things settle.\n    53→\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n    54→\n    55→\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n    56→\t// while debouncing.
