If events keep\n    57→\t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\n    58→\tdebounceMax time.Duration\n    59→\n    60→\t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\n    61→\tenableEDSDebounce bool\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→// XDSServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\n    65→type XDSServer struct {\n    66→\t// Env is the model environment.\n    67→\tEnv *model.Environment\n    68→\n    69→\t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\n    70→\t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\n    71→\t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\n    72→\t// different generator for a type.\n    73→\t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\n    74→\tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\n    75→\n    76→\t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators\n    77→\t// may also choose to not send any updates.\n    78→\tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\n    79→\n    80→\t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\n    81→\tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\n    82→\t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n    83→\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n    84→\n    85→\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n    86→\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n    87→\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n    88→\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\n    89→\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\n    90→\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\n    91→\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\n    92→\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\n    93→\n    94→\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\n    95→\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\n    96→\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\n    97→\n    98→\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\n    99→\tpushQueue *PushQueue\n   100→\n   101→\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\n   102→\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\n   103→\n   104→\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\n   105→\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\n   106→\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\n   107→\n   108→\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. Should be same/subset of the CA authenticators.\n   109→\tAuthenticators []security.Authenticator\n   110→\n   111→\tWorkloadEntryController *autoregistration.Controller\n   112→\n   113→\t// serverReady indicates caches have been synced up and server is ready to process requests.\n   114→\tserverReady atomic.Bool\n   115→\n   116→\tDebounceOptions DebounceOptions\n   117→\n   118→\t// Cache for XDS resources\n   119→\tCache model.XdsCache\n   120→\n   121→\t// JwtKeyResolver holds a reference to the JWT key resolver instance.\n   122→\tJwtKeyResolver *model.JwksResolver\n   123→\n   124→\t// ListRemoteClusters collects debug information about other clusters this istiod reads from.\n   125→\tListRemoteClusters func() []cluster.DebugInfo\n   126→\n   127→\t// ClusterAliases are alias names for cluster. When a proxy connects with a cluster ID\n   128→\t// and if it has a different alias we should use that a cluster ID for proxy.\n   129→\tClusterAliases map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID\n   130→\n   131→\t// pushVersion stores the numeric push version. This should be accessed via NextVersion()\n   132→\tpushVersion atomic.Uint64\n   133→\n   134→\t// DiscoveryStartTime is the time since the binary started\n   135→\tDiscoveryStartTime time.Time\n   136→\n   137→\tkrtDebugger *krt.DebugHandler\n   138→}\n   139→\n   140→// NewXDSServer creates XDSServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures\n   141→func NewXDSServer(env *model.Environment, clusterAliases map[string]string, debugger *krt.DebugHandler) *XDSServer {\n   142→\tout := &XDSServer{\n   143→\t\tEnv:                 env,\n   144→\t\tGenerators:          map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator{},\n   145→\t\tProxyNeedsPush:      DefaultProxyNeedsPush,\n   146→\t\tconcurrentPushLimit: make(chan struct{}, features.PushThrottle),\n   147→\t\tRequestRateLimit:    rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(features.RequestLimit), 1),\n   148→\t\tInboundUpdates:      atomic.NewInt64(0),\n   149→\t\tCommittedUpdates:    atomic.NewInt64(0),\n   150→\t\tpushChannel:         make(chan *model.PushRequest, 10),\n   151→\t\tpushQueue:           NewPushQueue(),\n   152→\t\tdebugHandlers:       map[string]string{},\n   153→\t\tadsClients:          map[string]*Connection{},\n   154→\t\tkrtDebugger:         debugger,\n   155→\t\tDebounceOptions: DebounceOptions{\n   156→\t\t\tDebounceAfter:     features.DebounceAfter,\n   157→\t\t\tdebounceMax:       features.DebounceMax,\n   158→\t\t\tenableEDSDebounce: features.EnableEDSDebounce,\n   159→\t\t},\n   160→\t\tCache:              env.Cache,\n   161→\t\tDiscoveryStartTime: processStartTime,\n   162→\t}\n   163→\n   164→\tout.ClusterAliases = make(map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID)\n   165→\tfor alias := range clusterAliases {\n   166→\t\tout.ClusterAliases[cluster.ID(alias)] = cluster.ID(clusterAliases[alias])\n   167→\t}\n   168→\n   169→\tout.initJwksResolver()\n   170→\n   171→\treturn out\n   172→}\n   173→\n   174→// initJwkResolver initializes the JWT key resolver to be used.\n   175→func (s *XDSServer) initJwksResolver() {\n   176→\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\n   177→\t\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n   178→\t}\n   179→\ts.JwtKeyResolver = model.NewJwksResolver(\n   180→\t\tmodel.JwtPubKeyEvictionDuration, model.JwtPubKeyRefreshInterval,\n   181→\t\tmodel.JwtPubKeyRefreshIntervalOnFailure, model.JwtPubKeyRetryInterval)\n   182→\n   183→\t// Flush cached discovery responses when detecting jwt public key change.\n   184→\ts.JwtKeyResolver.PushFunc = func() {\n   185→\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger), Forced: true})\n   186→\t}\n   187→}\n   188→\n   189→// closeJwksResolver shuts down the JWT key resolver used.\n   190→func (s *XDSServer) closeJwksResolver() {\n   191→\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\n   192→\t\ts.JwtKeyResolver.Close()\n   193→\t}\n   194→}\n   195→\n   196→// Register adds the ADS handler to the grpc server\n   197→func (s *XDSServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\n   198→\t// Register v3 server\n   199→\tdiscovery.RegisterAggregatedDiscoveryServiceServer(rpcs, s)\n   200→}\n   201→\n   202→var processStartTime = time.Now()\n   203→\n   204→// CachesSynced is called when caches have been synced so that server can accept connections.\n   205→func (s *XDSServer) CachesSynced() {\n   206→\tlog.Infof(\"All caches have been synced up in %v, marking server ready\", time.Since(s.DiscoveryStartTime))\n   207→\ts.serverReady.Store(true)\n   208→}\n   209→\n   210→func (s *XDSServer) IsServerReady() bool {\n   211→\treturn s.serverReady.Load()\n   212→}\n   213→\n   214→func (s *XDSServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   215→\tgo s.WorkloadEntryController.Run(stopCh)\n   216→\tgo s.handleUpdates(stopCh)\n   217→\tgo s.periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh)\n   218→\tgo s.sendPushes(stopCh)\n   219→\tgo s.Cache.Run(stopCh)\n   220→}\n   221→\n   222→// Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\n   223→func (s *XDSServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   224→\tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\n   225→\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n   226→\tfor {\n   227→\t\tselect {\n   228→\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n   229→\t\t\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\n   230→\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n   231→\t\t\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\n   232→\t\t\t\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\n   233→\t\t\t\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\n   234→\t\t\t\tout, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n   235→\t\t\t\tif string(out) != \"{}\" {\n   236→\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push Status: %s\", string(out))\n   237→\t\t\t\t}\n   238→\t\t\t}\n   239→\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n   240→\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n   241→\t\t\treturn\n   242→\t\t}\n   243→\t}\n   244→}\n   245→\n   246→// dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request\n   247→func (s *XDSServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   248→\t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\n   249→\tif req.Forced {\n   250→\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n   251→\t} else {\n   252→\t\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\n   253→\t\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\n   254→\t}\n   255→}\n   256→\n   257→// Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\n   258→func (s *XDSServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   259→\tif !req.Full {\n   260→\t\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\n   261→\t\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n   262→\t\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n   263→\t\treturn\n   264→\t}\n   265→\t// Reset the status during the push.\n   266→\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\n   267→\tif oldPushContext != nil {\n   268→\t\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\n   269→\t\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\n   270→\t\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\n   271→\t}\n   272→\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\n   273→\t// saved.\n   274→\tt0 := time.Now()\n   275→\tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\n   276→\tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\n   277→\tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\n   278→\tlog.Debugf(\"InitContext %v for push took %s\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\n   279→\tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\n   280→\n   281→\treq.Push = push\n   282→\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n   283→}\n   284→\n   285→func nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\n   286→\treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\n   287→}\n   288→\n   289→// Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\n   290→// PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\n   291→// only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\n   292→func (s *XDSServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n   293→\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n   294→}\n   295→\n   296→var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n   297→\n   298→// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\n   299→func (s *XDSServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   300→\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n   301→\t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n   302→\t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n   303→\t\t}\n   304→\t}\n   305→\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n   306→\t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n   307→\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n   308→\t\t// the cache.\n   309→\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n   310→\t}\n   311→\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n   312→\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n   313→\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n   314→\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n   315→\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n   316→\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n   317→\t}\n   318→\ts.pushChannel <- req\n   319→}\n   320→\n   321→// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n   322→// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n   323→// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n   324→// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n   325→// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\n   326→func (s *XDSServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   327→\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n   328→}\n   329→\n   330→// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\n   331→func debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n   332→\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n   333→\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n   334→\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n   335→\n   336→\tpushCounter := 0\n   337→\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n   338→\n   339→\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n   340→\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n   341→\n   342→\tfree := true\n   343→\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n   344→\n   345→\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n   346→\t\tpushFn(req)\n   347→\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n   348→\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n   349→\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n   350→\t}\n   351→\n   352→\tpushWorker := func() {\n   353→\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n   354→\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n   355→\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n   356→\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n   357→\t\t\tif req != nil {\n   358→\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n   359→\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n   360→\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n   361→\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n   362→\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n   363→\t\t\t\t} else {\n   364→\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n   365→\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n   366→\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n   367→\t\t\t\t}\n   368→\t\t\t\tfree = false\n   369→\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n   370→\t\t\t\treq = nil\n   371→\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n   372→\t\t\t}\n   373→\t\t} else {\n   374→\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n   375→\t\t}\n   376→\t}\n   377→\n   378→\tfor {\n   379→\t\tselect {\n   380→\t\tcase <-freeCh:\n   381→\t\t\tfree = true\n   382→\t\t\tpushWorker()\n   383→\t\tcase r := <-ch:\n   384→\t\t\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\n   385→\t\t\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\n   386→\t\t\t\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\n   387→\t\t\t}\n   388→\t\t\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\n   389→\t\t\t\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\n   390→\t\t\t\tgo func(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   391→\t\t\t\t\tpushFn(req)\n   392→\t\t\t\t\tupdateSent.Inc()\n   393→\t\t\t\t}(r)\n   394→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   395→\t\t\t}\n   396→\n   397→\t\t\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\n   398→\t\t\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\n   399→\t\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\n   400→\t\t\t\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\n   401→\t\t\t}\n   402→\t\t\tdebouncedEvents++\n   403→\n   404→\t\t\treq = req.Merge(r)\n   405→\t\tcase <-timeChan:\n   406→\t\t\tif free {\n   407→\t\t\t\tpushWorker()\n   408→\t\t\t}\n   409→\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n   410→\t\t\treturn\n   411→\t\t}\n   412→\t}\n   413→}\n   414→\n   415→func configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n   416→\tconfigs := \"\"\n   417→\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n   418→\t\tconfigs += key.String()\n   419→\t\tbreak\n   420→\t}\n   421→\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\n   422→\t\tmore := \" and \" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \" more configs\"\n   423→\t\tconfigs += more\n   424→\t}\n   425→\treturn configs\n   426→}\n   427→\n   428→func reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n   429→\tvar (\n   430→\t\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\n   431→\t\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\n   432→\t)\n   433→\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n   434→\t\tif idx == 0 {\n   435→\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n   436→\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n   437→\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n   438→\t\t} else {\n   439→\t\t\tbreak\n   440→\t\t}\n   441→\t\tidx++\n   442→\t}\n   443→\n   444→\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\n   445→\tcase 0:\n   446→\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n   447→\tcase 1:\n   448→\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\n   449→\tcase 2:\n   450→\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n   451→\tdefault:\n   452→\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n   453→\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n   454→\t}\n   455→}\n   456→\n   457→func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n   458→\tfor {\n   459→\t\tselect {\n   460→\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n   461→\t\t\treturn\n   462→\t\tdefault:\n   463→\t\t\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\n   464→\t\t\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\n   465→\t\t\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\n   466→\n   467→\t\t\t// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.\n   468→\t\t\tclient, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()\n   469→\t\t\tif shuttingdown {\n   470→\t\t\t\treturn\n   471→\t\t\t}\n   472→\t\t\trecordPushTriggers(push.Reason)\n   473→\t\t\t// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.\n   474→\t\t\tdoneFunc := func() {\n   475→\t\t\t\tqueue.MarkDone(client)\n   476→\t\t\t\t<-semaphore\n   477→\t\t\t}\n   478→\n   479→\t\t\tproxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())\n   480→\t\t\tvar closed <-chan struct{}\n   481→\t\t\tif client.deltaStream != nil {\n   482→\t\t\t\tclosed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()\n   483→\t\t\t} else {\n   484→\t\t\t\tclosed = client.StreamDone()\n   485→\t\t\t}\n   486→\t\t\tgo func() {\n   487→\t\t\t\tpushEv := &Event{\n   488→\t\t\t\t\tpushRequest: push,\n   489→\t\t\t\t\tdone:        doneFunc,\n   490→\t\t\t\t}\n   491→\n   492→\t\t\t\tselect {\n   493→\t\t\t\tcase client.PushCh() <- pushEv:\n   494→\t\t\t\t\treturn\n   495→\t\t\t\tcase <-closed: // grpc stream was closed\n   496→\t\t\t\t\tdoneFunc()\n   497→\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Client closed connection %v\", client.ID())\n   498→\t\t\t\t}\n   499→\t\t\t}()\n   500→\t\t}\n   501→\t}\n   502→}\n   503→\n   504→// initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this\n   505→// method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\n   506→// if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\n   507→// reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\n   508→func (s *XDSServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\n   509→\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\n   510→\tpush.PushVersion = version\n   511→\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\n   512→\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\n   513→\n   514→\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n   515→\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\n   516→\n   517→\treturn push\n   518→}\n   519→\n   520→func (s *XDSServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   521→\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\n   522→}\n   523→\n   524→// Shutdown shuts down XDSServer components.\n   525→func (s *XDSServer) Shutdown() {\n   526→\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n   527→\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\n   528→}\n   529→\n   530→// Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\n   531→// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\n   532→// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\n   533→func (s *XDSServer) Clients() []*Connection {\n   534→\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n   535→\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n   536→\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\n   537→\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\n   538→\t\tselect {\n   539→\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n   540→\t\tdefault:\n   541→\t\t\t// Initialization not complete, skip\n   542→\t\t\tcontinue\n   543→\t\t}\n   544→\t\tclients = append(clients, con)\n   545→\t}\n   546→\treturn clients\n   547→}\n   548→\n   549→// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\n   550→// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\n   551→func (s *XDSServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\n   552→\tclients := s.Clients()\n   553→\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\n   554→\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\n   555→\t\t\treturn true\n   556→\t\t}\n   557→\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\n   558→\t\t\treturn true\n   559→\t\t}\n   560→\t\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\n   561→\t})\n   562→\treturn clients\n   563→}\n   564→\n   565→// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\n   566→// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\n   567→func (s *XDSServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\n   568→\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n   569→\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n   570→\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\n   571→}\n   572→\n   573→func (s *XDSServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\n   574→\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\n   575→\t\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\n   576→\t\treturn nil\n   577→\t}\n   578→\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\n   579→\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\n   580→\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\n   581→\tdefer cancel()\n   582→\treturn s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)\n   583→}\n   584→\n   585→func (s *XDSServer) NextVersion() string {\n   586→\treturn time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + \"/\" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)\n   587→}\n   588→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. \\n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\\\"K_REVISION\\\", \\\"\\\",\\n57: \\t\\\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\\\").Get()\\n58: \\n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\\n61: \\n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\\n63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\\n64: \\n65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\\n66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\\n67: \\n68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\\n69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\\n70: \\n71: type Connection struct {\\n72: \\txds.Connection\\n73: \\n74: \\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\\n75: \\t// This is included in internal events.\\n76: \\tnode *core.Node\\n77: \\n78: \\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\\n79: \\tproxy *model.Proxy\\n80: \\n81: \\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\\n82: \\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\\n83: \\n84: \\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\\n85: \\n86: \\ts   *DiscoveryServer\\n87: \\tids []string\\n88: }\\n89: \\n90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\\n91: \\treturn &conn.Connection\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\\n95: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n96: }\\n97: \\n98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\\n99: type Event struct {\\n100: \\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\\n101: \\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\\n102: \\n103: \\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\\n104: \\tdone func()\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\\n108: \\treturn &Connection{\\n109: \\t\\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\\n110: \\t}\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\\n114: \\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\\n118: \\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\\n122: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\\n126: \\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\\n130: \\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\\n131: \\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\\n132: \\tpushEv.done()\\n133: \\treturn err\\n134: }\\n135: \\n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n140: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n141: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \\\", stype,\\n142: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\\n143: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n144: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\\n145: \\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\\n149: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n150: \\t\\treturn s.pushXds(con,\\n151: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\\n156: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n161: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n162: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n163: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n166: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n167: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n168: \\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n169: \\t\\tDelta:  delta,\\n170: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\\n174: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n175: \\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\\n176: \\t// computation.\\n177: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n178: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n185: \\treturn s.Stream(stream)\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n189: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n190: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n191: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n192: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n193: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n194: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n195: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n198: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n199: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n200: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n201: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n202: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n203: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n204: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n205: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n206: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\n209: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n210: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n211: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n212: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\n215: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n217: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n221: \\tif err != nil {\\n222: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\tif ids != nil {\\n225: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\\\", peerAddr)\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n231: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n232: \\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n233: \\tcon.ids = ids\\n234: \\tcon.s = s\\n235: \\treturn xds.Stream(con)\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\\n239: // to the tracking map.\\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n241: \\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\\n242: \\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\\n247: \\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\\n248: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\\n251: \\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\\n252: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\\n253: \\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\\n254: \\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\\n255: \\tcon.node = node\\n256: \\tcon.proxy = proxy\\n257: \\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\\n258: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\\\")\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\t// Authorize xds clients\\n262: \\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\treturn err\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\\n267: \\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\\n268: \\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\\n269: \\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\\n270: \\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\\n271: \\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\\n272: \\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\\n273: \\t// proxy\\n274: \\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\\n275: \\n276: \\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\\n277: \\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\\n278: \\t\\ts.closeConnection(con)\\n279: \\t\\treturn err\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\\n285: \\tif con.ID() == \\\"\\\" {\\n286: \\t\\treturn\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\\n289: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\\n293: \\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\\n294: \\treturn node + \\\"-\\\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\\n301: \\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\\n302: \\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\\n310: \\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\\n311: \\tproxy.XdsNode = node\\n312: \\treturn proxy, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\\n318: \\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\\n319: \\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\\n320: \\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\\n321: \\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\\n322: \\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\\n323: \\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\\n325: \\t\\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\\n326: \\t\\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\\n327: \\t\\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\\n328: \\t\\t\\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\\n329: \\t\\t\\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\\n330: \\t\\t\\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\\n331: \\t\\t}\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\\n334: \\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\\n335: \\t\\tproxy.Labels,\\n336: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n337: \\t\\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\\n338: \\t\\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\\n339: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\\n340: \\t)\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\\n344: \\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\\n345: \\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\\n346: \\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\\n347: \\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\\n348: \\t\\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. It is expected to be called only after\\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\\n366: \\tproxy := con.proxy\\n367: \\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\\n368: \\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\\n369: \\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\\n370: \\t\\treturn err\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\\n373: \\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\\n374: \\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\\n375: \\n376: \\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\\n377: \\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\\n378: \\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \\\"\\\" {\\n379: \\t\\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\treturn nil\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\\n386: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n387: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n388: \\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\\n389: \\t// 1. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\\n409: \\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\\n410: \\t// applicable to this proxy\\n411: \\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\\n412: \\tif request == nil {\\n413: \\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n414: \\t} else {\\n415: \\t\\tpush = request.Push\\n416: \\t\\tif request.Forced {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\\n420: \\t\\t\\tswitch conf.Kind {\\n421: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\\n422: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n423: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Gateway:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n425: shouldResetGateway {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\t\\t}\\n433: \\t}\\n434: \\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\\n435: \\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\\n436: \\t\\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\\n437: \\t}\\n438: \\t// only compute gateways for \\\"router\\\" type proxy.\\n439: \\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\\n440: \\t\\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\\n443: \\tif request != nil {\\n444: \\t\\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\\n445: \\t}\\n446: }\\n447: \\n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req }\\n459: \\n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\\n462: // This works if both ends This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n140: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n141: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \\\", stype,\\n142: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\\n143: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n144: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\\n145: \\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\\n149: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n150: \\t\\treturn s.pushXds(con,\\n151: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\\n156: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n161: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n162: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n163: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n166: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n167: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n168: \\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n169: \\t\\tDelta:  delta,\\n170: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\\n174: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n175: \\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\\n176: \\t// computation.\\n177: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n178: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n185: \\treturn s.Stream(stream)\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n189: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n190: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n191: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n192: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n193: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n194: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n195: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n198: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n199: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n200: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n201: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n202: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n203: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n204: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n205: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n206: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\n209: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n210: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n211: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n212: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\n215: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n217: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n221: \\tif err != nil {\\n222: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\tif ids != nil {\\n225: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\\\", peerAddr)\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n231: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n232: \\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n233: \\tcon.ids = ids\\n234: \\tcon.s = s\\n235: \\treturn xds.Stream(con)\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\\n239: // to the tracking map.\\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n241: \\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\\n242: \\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\\n247: \\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\\n248: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\\n251: \\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\\n252: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\\n253: \\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\\n254: \\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\\n255: \\tcon.node = node\\n256: \\tcon.proxy = proxy\\n257: \\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\\n258: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\\\")\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\t// Authorize xds clients\\n262: \\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\treturn err\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\\n267: \\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\\n268: \\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\\n269: \\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\\n270: \\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\\n271: \\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\\n272: \\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\\n273: \\t// proxy\\n274: \\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\\n275: \\n276: \\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\\n277: \\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\\n278: \\t\\ts.closeConnection(con)\\n279: \\t\\treturn err\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\\n285: \\tif con.ID() == \\\"\\\" {\\n286: \\t\\treturn\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\\n289: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\\n293: \\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\\n294: \\treturn node + \\\"-\\\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\\n301: \\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\\n302: \\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\\n310: \\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\\n311: \\tproxy.XdsNode = node\\n312: \\treturn proxy, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\\n318: \\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\\n319: \\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\\n320: \\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\\n321: \\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\\n322: \\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\\n323: \\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\\n325: \\t\\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\\n326: \\t\\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\\n327: \\t\\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\\n328: \\t\\t\\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\\n329: \\t\\t\\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\\n330: \\t\\t\\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\\n331: \\t\\t}\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\\n334: \\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\\n335: \\t\\tproxy.Labels,\\n336: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n337: \\t\\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\\n338: \\t\\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\\n339: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\\n340: \\t)\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\\n344: \\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\\n345: \\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\\n346: \\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\\n347: \\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\\n348: \\t\\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\\n349: \\t\\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\\n350: \\t\\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\\n351: \\t\\tif localityLabel != \\\"\\\" {\\n352: \\t\\t\\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\\n353: \\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\treturn &core.Locality{\\n357: \\t\\tRegion:  region,\\n358: \\t\\tZone:    zone,\\n359: \\t\\tSubZone: subzone,\\n360: \\t}\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\\n366: \\tproxy := con.proxy\\n367: \\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\\n368: \\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\\n369: \\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\\n370: \\t\\treturn err\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\\n373: \\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\\n374: \\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\\n375: \\n376: \\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\\n377: \\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\\n378: \\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \\\"\\\" {\\n379: \\t\\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\treturn nil\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\\n386: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n387: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n388: \\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\\n389: \\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\\n390: \\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\\n391: \\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\\n392: \\t\\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\\n393: \\t\\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\\n394: \\t\\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\\n395: \\t\\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\\n396: \\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\t// only recompute workload labels when\\n400: \\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\\n401: \\t// 2. proxy update\\n402: \\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\\n403: \\tif recomputeLabels {\\n404: \\t\\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\\n405: \\t\\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\\n406: \\t}\\n407: \\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\\n408: \\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\\n409: \\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\\n410: \\t// applicable to this proxy\\n411: \\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\\n412: \\tif request == nil {\\n413: \\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n414: \\t} else {\\n415: \\t\\tpush = request.Push\\n416: \\t\\tif request.Forced {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\\n420: \\t\\t\\tswitch conf.Kind {\\n421: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\\n422: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n423: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Gateway:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n425: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Ingress:\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\t\\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\t\\t}\\n433: \\t}\\n434: \\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\\n435: \\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\\n436: \\t\\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\\n437: \\t}\\n438: \\t// only compute gateways for \\\"router\\\" type proxy.\\n439: \\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\\n440: \\t\\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\\n443: \\tif request != nil {\\n444: \\t\\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\\n445: \\t}\\n446: }\\n447: \\n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\\n450: \\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\\n451: \\t\\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\\n452: \\t\\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\\n453: \\t\\tif !event.Healthy {\\n454: \\t\\t\\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\\n457: \\t}\\n458: }\\n459: \\n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\\n462: // This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\\n463: // pattern.\\n464: //\\n465: // The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\\n466: // list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\\n467: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\\n468: \\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\\n472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\\n473: \\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\\n474: \\n475: \\tif pushRequest.Full {\\n476: \\t\\t// Update Proxy with current information.\\n477: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n478: \\t}\\n479: \\n480: \\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n481: \\tif !needsPush {\\n482: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\\\", con.ID())\\n483: \\t\\treturn nil\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\t// Send pushes to all generators\\n487: \\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\\n488: \\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\\n489: \\tfor _, w := range wrl {\\n490: \\t\\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\t}\\n494: \\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\\n495: \\treturn nil\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: // PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\\n499: // order after the types listed here\\n500: var PushOrder = []string{\\n501: \\tv3.ClusterType,\\n502: \\tv3.EndpointType,\\n503: \\tv3.ListenerType,\\n504: \\tv3.RouteType,\\n505: \\tv3.SecretType,\\n506: \\tv3.AddressType,\\n507: \\tv3.WorkloadType,\\n508: \\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\\n509: }\\n510: \\n511: // \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n516: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n517: \\treturn len(s.adsClients)\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip \\t})\\n548: }\\n549: \\n550: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\\n551: // Mainly used in Debug interface.\\n552: func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\\n553: {\\n563: \\tif !req.Full {\\n564: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\\\",\\n565: \\t\\t\\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\\n566: \\t} else {\\n567: req.Push.PushVersion)\\n570: \\t\\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\\n571: \\n572: \\t\\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\\n573: \\t\\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\\n574: event.\\n582: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n583: \\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\\n584: \\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n585: \\t\\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\\n586: \\t\\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\\n587: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\\\", currentlyPending)\\n588: \\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\\n601: }\\n602: \\n603: func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\\n604: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\\n605: \\tdefer for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\\n622: func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\\n623: \\tallWatched := to handle the load balancing behavior.\n54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\n55: \n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\"K_REVISION\", \"\",\n57: \t\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\").Get()\n58: \n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n61: \n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n70: \n71: type Connection struct {\n72: \txds.Connection\n73: \n74: \t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n75: \t// This is included in internal events.\n76: \tnode *core.Node\n77: \n78: \t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n79: \tproxy *model.Proxy\n80: \n81: \t// deltaStream is This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n104: \tdone func()\n105: }\n106: \n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n108: \treturn &Connection{\n109: \t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n110: \t}\n111: }\n112: \n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n114: \treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n115: }\n116: \n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\n118: \tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n119: }\n120: \n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n122: \treturn conn.proxy\n123: }\n124: \n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n126: \treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n127: }\n128: \n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n130: \tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n131: \terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n132: \tpushEv.done()\n133: \treturn err\n134: }\n135: \n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n140: \tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n141: \tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n142: \t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n143: \tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n144: \t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n145: \t\treturn nil\n146: \t}\n147: \n148: \t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n149: \tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n150: \t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n151: \t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n152: \t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n153: \t}\n154: \n155: \tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n156: \tif !shouldRespond {\n157: \t\treturn nil\n158: \t}\n159: \n160: \trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n161: \t\tFull:   true,\n162: \t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n163: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n164: \n165: \t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n166: \t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n167: \t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n168: \t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n169: \t\tDelta:  delta,\n170: \t\tForced: true,\n171: \t}\n172: \n173: \t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n174: \t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n175: \t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n176: \t// computation.\n177: \tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n178: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n179: \t}\n180: \treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n181: }\n182: \n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n185: \treturn s.Stream(stream)\n186: }\n187: \n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n189: \tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n190: \t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n191: \t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n192: \t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n193: \t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n194: \t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n195: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n196: \t}\n197: \t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n198: \t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n199: \t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n200: \t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n201: \t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n202: \t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n203: \t// ip tables update latencies.\n204: \t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n205: \tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n206: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n207: \t}\n208: \n209: \tctx := stream.Context()\n210: \tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n211: \tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n212: \t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n213: \t}\n214: \n215: \tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n216: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n217: \t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n218: \t}\n219: \n220: \tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n221: \tif err != nil {\n222: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n223: \t}\n224: \tif ids != nil {\n225: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n226: \t} else {\n227: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n228: \t}\n229: \n230: \t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n231: \ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n232: \tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n233: \tcon.ids = ids\n234: \tcon.s = s\n235: \treturn xds.Stream(con)\n236: }\n237: \n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n239: // to the tracking map.\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n241: \t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n242: \tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n243: \tif err != nil {\n244: \t\treturn err\n245: \t}\n246: \t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n247: \tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n248: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n249: \t}\n250: \t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n251: \t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n252: \tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n253: \t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n254: \tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n255: \tcon.node = node\n256: \tcon.proxy = proxy\n257: \tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n258: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n259: \t}\n260: \n261: \t// Authorize xds clients\n262: \tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n263: \t\treturn err\n264: \t}\n265: \n266: \t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n267: \t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n268: \t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n269: \t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n270: \t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n271: \ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n272: \t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n273: \t// proxy\n274: \tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n275: \n276: \t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n277: \tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n278: \t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n279: \t\treturn err\n280: \t}\n281: \treturn nil\n282: }\n283: \n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n285: \tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n286: \t\treturn\n287: \t}\n288: \ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n289: \ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n290: }\n291: \n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\n293: \tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n294: \treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n295: }\n296: \n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n301: \tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n302: \tif err != nil {\n303: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n304: \t}\n305: \tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n306: \tif err != nil {\n307: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n308: \t}\n309: \t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n310: \tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n311: \tproxy.XdsNode = node\n312: \treturn proxy, nil\n313: }\n314: \n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n318: \t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n319: \t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n320: \t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n321: \t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n322: \tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n323: \tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n324: \t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n325: \t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n326: \t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n327: \t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n328: \t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n329: \t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n330: \t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n331: \t\t}\n332: \t}\n333: \t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n334: \tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n335: \t\tproxy.Labels,\n336: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n337: \t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n338: \t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n339: \t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n340: \t)\n341: }\n342: \n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n344: \tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n345: \tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n346: \tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n347: \tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n348: \t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n349: \t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n350: \t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n351: \t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n352: \t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n353: \t\t}\n354: \t\treturn nil\n355: \t}\n356: \treturn &core.Locality{\n357: \t\tRegion:  region,\n358: \t\tZone:    zone,\n359: \t\tSubZone: subzone,\n360: \t}\n361: }\n362: \n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy.
