This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\nvar KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) adsClientCount() int {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *XDSServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t} else {\n\t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n\n\t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t} else {\n\t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n\t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n}\n\n// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then add any undeclared types\n\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n\t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn ordered\n}\nADS_FILE\n\necho the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n28: \\n29: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n30: \\tistiogrpc \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n33: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n34: \\tistiolog \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n36: \\t_ \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\\\" // Ensure we get the more efficient vtproto gRPC encoder\\n37: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n39: )\\n40: \\n41: var deltaLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\\\"delta\\\", \\\"delta xds debugging\\\")\\n42: \\n43: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\\n44: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n45: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n46: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n47: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n48: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n49: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n50: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n51: \\t}\\n52: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n53: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n54: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n55: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n56: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n57: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n58: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n59: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n60: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n61: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\n64: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n65: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n66: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n67: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n68: \\t}\\n69: \\n70: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n71: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n72: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n73: \\t}\\n74: \\n75: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n76: \\tif err != nil {\\n77: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\tif ids != nil {\\n80: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n81: \\t} else {\\n82: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %v\\\", peerAddr)\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\n85: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n86: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n87: \\tcon := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected\\n90: \\t// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions\\n91: \\t// with push. According to the spec: \\\"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\\n92: \\t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\\\"\\n93: \\n94: \\t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\\n95: \\t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\\n96: \\tgo s.receiveDelta(con, ids)\\n97: \\n98: \\t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because\\n99: \\t// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout\\n100: \\t// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to\\n101: \\t// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the\\n102: \\t// initialization is complete.\\n103: \\t<-con.InitializedCh()\\n104: \\n105: \\tfor {\\n106: \\t\\t// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.\\n107: \\t\\t// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)\\n108: \\t\\t// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.\\n109: \\t\\tselect {\\n110: \\t\\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\\n111: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n112: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\\n113: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n114: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n115: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n116: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\\n118: \\t\\t\\t}\\n119: \\t\\tcase <-con.StopCh():\\n120: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n121: \\t\\tdefault:\\n122: \\t\\t}\\n123: \\t\\t// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge\\n124: \\t\\t// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;\\n125: \\t\\t// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests\\n126: \\t\\t// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.\\n127: \\t\\tselect {\\n128: \\t\\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\\n129: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n130: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\\n131: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n133: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\\n135: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\\n136: \\t\\t\\t}\\n137: \\t\\tcase ev := <-con.PushCh():\\n138: \\t\\t\\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\\n139: \\t\\t\\terr := s.pushConnectionDelta(con, pushEv)\\n140: \\t\\t\\tpushEv.done()\\n141: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n142: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n143: \\t\\t\\t}\\n144: \\t\\tcase <-con.StopCh():\\n145: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t\\t}\\n147: \\t}\\n148: }\\n149: \\n150: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\\n151: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnectionDelta(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\\n152: \\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\\n153: \\n154: \\tif pushRequest.Full {\\n155: \\t\\t// Update Proxy with current information.\\n156: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\n159: \\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n160: \\tif !needsPush {\\n161: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\\\", con.ID())\\n162: \\t\\treturn nil\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\n165: \\t// Send pushes to all generators\\n166: \\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\\n167: \\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\\n168: \\tfor _, w := range wrl {\\n169: \\t\\tif err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\\n170: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n171: \\t\\t}\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\n174: \\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\\n175: \\treturn nil\\n176: }\\n177: \\n178: func (s *DiscoveryServer) receiveDelta(con *Connection, identities []string) {\\n179: \\tdefer func() {\\n180: \\t\\tclose(con.deltaReqChan)\\n181: \\t\\tclose(con.ErrorCh())\\n182: \\t\\t// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream\\n183: \\t\\tselect {\\n184: \\t\\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\\n185: \\t\\tdefault:\\n186: \\t\\t\\tclose(con.InitializedCh())\\n187: \\t\\t}\\n188: \\t}()\\n189: \\tfirstRequest := true\\n190: \\tfor {\\n191: \\t\\treq, err := con.deltaStream.Recv()\\n192: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n193: \\t\\t\\tif istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {\\n194: \\t\\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n195: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n196: \\t\\t\\t}\\n197: \\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\\n198: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID(), err)\\n199: \\t\\t\\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\\n200: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n201: \\t\\t}\\n202: \\t\\t// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.\\n203: \\t\\tif firstRequest {\\n204: \\t\\t\\t// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request\\n205: \\t\\t\\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n208: \\t\\t\\t}\\n209: \\t\\t\\tfirstRequest = false\\n210: \\t\\t\\tif req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == \\\"\\\" {\\n211: \\t\\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, \\\"missing node information\\\").Err()\\n212: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n213: \\t\\t\\t}\\n214: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {\\n215: \\t\\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n217: \\t\\t\\t}\\n218: \\t\\t\\tdefer s.closeConnection(con)\\n219: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: new delta connection for node:%s\\\", con.ID())\\n220: \\t\\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\t\\tselect {\\n223: \\t\\tcase con.deltaReqChan <- req:\\n224: \\t\\tcase <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():\\n225: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n226: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n227: \\t\\t}\\n228: \\t}\\n229: }\\n230: \\n231: func (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {\\n232: \\tsendResonse := func() error {\\n233: \\t\\tstart := time.Now()\\n234: \\t\\tdefer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()\\n235: \\t\\treturn conn.deltaStream.Send(res)\\n236: \\t}\\n237: \\terr := sendResonse()\\n238: \\tif err == nil {\\n239: \\t\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n240: \\t\\t\\tconn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n241: \\t\\t\\t\\tif wr == nil {\\n242: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}\\n243: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\t// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though\\n245: \\t\\t\\t\\tif newResourceNames != nil {\\n246: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n248: \\t\\t\\t\\twr.NonceSent = res.Nonce\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\twr.LastSendTime = time.Now()\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn wr\\n254: \\t\\t\\t})\\n255: \\t\\t}\\n256: \\t} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\\n257: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"Timeout writing %s: %v\\\", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))\\n258: \\t\\txds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\treturn err\\n261: }\\n262: \\n263: // processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n264: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n265: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n266: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n267: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n268: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s\\\", stype,\\n269: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)\\n270: \\n271: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n272: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))\\n273: \\t\\treturn nil\\n274: \\t}\\n275: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n276: \\t\\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con,\\n277: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},\\n278: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n279: \\t}\\n280: \\n281: \\tshouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)\\n282: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n283: \\t\\treturn nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\tsubs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)\\n287: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n288: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n289: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n290: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n291: \\n292: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n293: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n294: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n295: \\t\\tStart: con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n296: \\t\\tDelta: model.ResourceDelta{\\n297: \\t\\t\\t// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info\\n298: \\t\\t\\tSubscribed:   subs,\\n299: \\t\\t\\tUnsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete(\\\"*\\\"),\\n300: \\t\\t},\\n301: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n302: \\t}\\n303: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.\\n304: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n305: \\t// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).\\n306: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n307: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\n310: \\terr := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n311: \\tif err != nil {\\n312: \\t\\treturn err\\n313: \\t}\\n314: \\t// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.\\n315: \\t// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if\\n316: \\t// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).\\n317: \\t// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,\\n318: \\t// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed\\n319: \\t// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues\\n320: \\t// with EDS and SDS.\\n321: \\t// This can happen with the following sequence\\n322: \\t// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.\\n323: \\t// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.\\n324: \\t// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.\\n325: \\t// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends\\n326: \\t//    an EDS request for them.\\n327: \\t// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response\\n328: \\t// \\t  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.\\n329: \\t// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.\\n330: \\tif req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {\\n331: \\t\\treturn nil\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\treturn s.forceEDSPush(con)\\n334: }\\n335: \\n336: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {\\n337: \\tif dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {\\n338: \\t\\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n339: \\t\\t\\tFull:   true,\\n340: \\t\\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n341: \\t\\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),\\n342: \\t\\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n343: \\t\\t\\tForced: true,\\n344: \\t\\t}\\n345: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.\\\", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())\\n346: \\t\\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\treturn nil\\n349: }\\n350: \\n351: // shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol\\n352: // using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.\\n353: func shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {\\n354: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)\\n355: \\n356: \\t// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.\\n357: \\t// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info\\n358: \\t// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.\\n359: \\tif request.ErrorDetail != nil {\\n360: \\t\\terrCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)\\n361: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s\\\", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())\\n362: \\t\\txds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())\\n363: \\t\\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n364: \\t\\t\\twr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn wr\\n366: \\t\\t})\\n367: \\t\\treturn false\\n368: \\t}\\n369: \\n370: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v\\\", stype, con.ID(),\\n371: \\t\\trequest.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)\\n372: \\tpreviousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)\\n373: \\n374: \\t// This can happen in two cases:\\n375: \\t// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod\\n376: \\t// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have\\n377: \\t// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either\\n378: \\t// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.\\n379: \\t// We should always respond with the current resource names.\\n380: \\tif previousInfo == nil {\\n381: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Lock()\\n382: \\t\\tdefer con.proxy.Unlock()\\n383: \\n384: \\t\\tif len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {\\n385: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, scale, we do not store ResourceName.\\n394: \\t\\t\\t// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.\\n395: \\t\\t\\tres = nil\\n396: \\t\\t}\\n397: \\t\\tcon.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{\\n398: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\\n399: \\t\\t\\tResourceNames: res,\\n400: \\t\\t\\tWildcard:      wildcard,\\n401: \\t\\t}\\n402: \\t\\treturn true\\n403: \\t}\\n404: \\n405: \\t// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.\\n406: \\t// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.\\n407: \\tif request.ResponseNonce != \\\"\\\" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {\\n408: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s\\\", stype,\\n409: \\t\\t\\tcon.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)\\n410: \\t\\txds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()\\n411: \\t\\treturn false\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\n414: \\t// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.\\n415: \\t// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.\\n416: \\t// In this case response_nonce is empty.\\n417: \\tspontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == \\\"\\\"\\n418: \\n419: \\tvar alwaysRespond bool\\n420: \\tvar subChanged bool\\n421: \\n422: \\t// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.\\n423: \\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n424: \\t\\twr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)\\n425: \\t\\tif !spontaneousReq {\\n426: \\t\\t\\t// Clear last error, we got an ACK.\\n427: \\t\\t\\t// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info in place.\\n428: \\t\\t\\twr.LastError = \\\"\\\"\\n429: \\t\\t\\twr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce\\n430: \\t\\t}\\n431: \\t\\talwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond\\n432: \\t\\twr.AlwaysRespond = false\\n433: \\t\\treturn wr\\n434: \\t})\\n435: \\n436: \\t// It is invalid in the below two cases:\\n437: \\t// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.\\n438: \\t// 2. %v\\\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)\\n441: \\t\\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n442: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\\\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))\\n443: \\t\\t}\\n444: \\t}\\n445: \\n446: \\t// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce\\n447: \\t// when it detects a new resource. We should just generate the\\n477: \\t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\\n478: \\t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\\n479: \\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\\n480: \\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\\n481: \\tvar logFiltered string\\n482: \\tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {\\n483: \\t\\t// Some types opt out of this and natively handle \\t\\t} else {\\n539: \\t\\t\\tnewResourceNames = resourceNamesSet(res)\\n540: \\t\\t}\\n541: \\t}\\n542: \\tif neverRemoveDelta(w.TypeUrl) {\\n543: \\t\\tresp.RemovedResources = nil\\n544: This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\\n39: //\\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\\n44: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n45: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n46: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n48: \\taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\\n49: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n50: \\tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\\n51: \\t\\t// Nothing changed...\\n52: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n53: \\t}\\n54: \\n55: \\tif !w.Wildcard {\\n56: \\t\\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\n59: \\treqAddresses := addresses\\n60: \\tif isReq {\\n61: \\t\\treqAddresses = nil\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)\\n64: \\t// Note: while \\\"removed\\\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\\n65: \\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\\n66: \\thave := sets.New[string]()\\n67: \\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\\n68: \\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\\n69: \\t\\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)\\n70: \\t}\\n71: \\n72: \\tif isReq {\\n73: \\t\\t// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.\\n74: \\t\\t// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.\\n75: \\t\\t// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:\\n76: \\t\\t// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).\\n77: \\t\\tremoved = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\n80: \\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.\\n81: \\t// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).\\n82: \\t// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from\\n83: \\t// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.\\n84: \\n85: \\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\\n86: }\\n87: \\n88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {\\n89: \\tn := addr.ResourceName()\\n90: \\thave.Insert(n)\\n91: \\tswitch requestedType {\\n92: \\tcase v3.WorkloadType:\\n93: \\t\\tif addr.GetWorkload() != nil {\\n94: \\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n96: \\t\\t\\t\\tAliases:  aliases,\\n97: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal\\n98: \\t\\t\\t})\\n99: \\t\\t}\\n100: \\tcase v3.AddressType:\\n101: \\t\\tproto := addr.Marshaled\\n102: \\t\\tif proto == nil {\\n103: \\t\\t\\tproto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)\\n104: \\t\\t}\\n105: \\n106: \\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n107: \\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n108: \\t\\t\\tAliases:  aliases,\\n109: \\t\\t\\tResource: proto,\\n110: \\t\\t})\\n111: \\t}\\n112: \\treturn resources\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: func (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n116: \\tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\\n117: \\treturn resources, details, err\\n118: }\\n119: \\n120: func (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(\\n121: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n122: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n123: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n124: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n125: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n126: \\tsubs := w.ResourceNames\\n127: \\n128: \\tvar addresses sets.String\\n129: \\tif isReq {\\n130: \\t\\t// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address\\n131: \\t\\t// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.\\n132: \\t\\t// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.\\n133: \\t\\taddresses = req.Delta.Subscribed\\n134: \\t} else {\\n135: \\t\\t// this is from the external triggers instead of request\\n136: \\t\\t// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated\\n137: \\t\\taddresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\t// We only need this for on-demand. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EzQkSFazvWVFoDpLJ3YtTK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Qt5dS8Jcm8oeSqxBMRx9bK","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/delta.go","content":"// Copyright Istio Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n\tistiogrpc \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n\tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n\tistiolog \"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\"\n\t_ \"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\" // Ensure we get the more efficient vtproto gRPC encoder\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n)\n\nvar deltaLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"delta\", \"delta xds debugging\")\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %v\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\n\t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected\n\t// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions\n\t// with push. According to the spec: \"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\n\t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\"\n\n\t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\n\t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\n\tgo s.receiveDelta(con, ids)\n\n\t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because\n\t// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout\n\t// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to\n\t// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the\n\t// initialization is complete.\n\t<-con.InitializedCh()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\t// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.\n\t\t// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)\n\t\t// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n\t\t\t\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-con.StopCh():\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge\n\t\t// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;\n\t\t// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests\n\t\t// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n\t\t\t\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase ev := <-con.PushCh():\n\t\t\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\t\t\terr := s.pushConnectionDelta(con, pushEv)\n\t\t\tpushEv.done()\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-con.StopCh():\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushConnectionDelta(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) receiveDelta(con *Connection, identities []string) {\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tclose(con.deltaReqChan)\n\t\tclose(con.ErrorCh())\n\t\t// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tclose(con.InitializedCh())\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tfirstRequest := true\n\tfor {\n\t\treq, err := con.deltaStream.Recv()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tif istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {\n\t\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v\", con.Peer(), con.ID(), err)\n\t\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.\n\t\tif firstRequest {\n\t\t\t// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request\n\t\t\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfirstRequest = false\n\t\t\tif req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, \"missing node information\").Err()\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer s.closeConnection(con)\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: new delta connection for node:%s\", con.ID())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase con.deltaReqChan <- req:\n\t\tcase <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {\n\tsendResonse := func() error {\n\t\tstart := time.Now()\n\t\tdefer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()\n\t\treturn conn.deltaStream.Send(res)\n\t}\n\terr := sendResonse()\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\t\tconn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n\t\t\t\tif wr == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\twr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though\n\t\t\t\tif newResourceNames != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\twr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\twr.NonceSent = res.Nonce\n\t\t\t\twr.LastSendTime = time.Now()\n\t\t\t\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\n\t\t\t\t\twr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn wr\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"Timeout writing %s: %v\", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))\n\t\txds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()\n\t}\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s\", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)\n\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tsubs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart: con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta: model.ResourceDelta{\n\t\t\t// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info\n\t\t\tSubscribed:   subs,\n\t\t\tUnsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete(\"*\"),\n\t\t},\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\n\terr := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.\n\t// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if\n\t// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).\n\t// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,\n\t// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed\n\t// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues\n\t// with EDS and SDS.\n\t// This can happen with the following sequence\n\t// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.\n\t// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.\n\t// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.\n\t// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends\n\t//    an EDS request for them.\n\t// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response\n\t// \t  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.\n\t// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.\n\tif req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn s.forceEDSPush(con)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {\n\tif dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {\n\t\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),\n\t\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\t\tForced: true,\n\t\t}\n\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.\", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())\n\t\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol\n// using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.\nfunc shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)\n\n\t// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.\n\t// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info\n\t// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.\n\tif request.ErrorDetail != nil {\n\t\terrCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)\n\t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s\", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())\n\t\txds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())\n\t\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n\t\t\twr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()\n\t\t\treturn wr\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v\", stype, con.ID(),\n\t\trequest.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)\n\tpreviousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)\n\n\t// This can happen in two cases:\n\t// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod\n\t// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have\n\t// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either\n\t// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.\n\t// We should always respond with the current resource names.\n\tif previousInfo == nil {\n\t\tcon.proxy.Lock()\n\t\tdefer con.proxy.Unlock()\n\n\t\tif len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, len(request.InitialResourceVersions))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: INIT %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tres, wildcard, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, request)\n\t\tskip := request.TypeUrl == v3.AddressType && wildcard\n\t\tif skip {\n\t\t\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we do not store ResourceName.\n\t\t\t// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.\n\t\t\tres = nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tcon.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\n\t\t\tResourceNames: res,\n\t\t\tWildcard:      wildcard,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.\n\t// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.\n\tif request.ResponseNonce != \"\" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s\", stype,\n\t\t\tcon.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)\n\t\txds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.\n\t// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.\n\t// In this case response_nonce is empty.\n\tspontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == \"\"\n\n\tvar alwaysRespond bool\n\tvar subChanged bool\n\n\t// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.\n\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n\t\twr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)\n\t\tif !spontaneousReq {\n\t\t\t// Clear last error, we got an ACK.\n\t\t\t// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info in place.\n\t\t\twr.LastError = \"\"\n\t\t\twr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce\n\t\t}\n\t\talwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond\n\t\twr.AlwaysRespond = false\n\t\treturn wr\n\t})\n\n\t// It is invalid in the below two cases:\n\t// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.\n\t// 2. subscribed resources changes from ACK.\n\tif spontaneousReq && !subChanged || !spontaneousReq && subChanged {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)\n\t\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce\n\t// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.\n\tif !subChanged {\n\t\t// We should always respond \"alwaysRespond\" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming\n\t\t// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.\n\t\tif alwaysRespond {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.\", stype, con.ID())\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: ACK %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// Push a Delta XDS resource for the given connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushDeltaXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\n\tif w == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\n\tif gen == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tt0 := time.Now()\n\n\toriginalW := w\n\t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. removal;\n\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\n\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\n\tvar logFiltered util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase !req.Full:\n\t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\",\n\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\tdebug := \"\"\n\t\tif This allows checking that the Delta XDS\n// response returned the optimal result. Checks include correctness checks (e.g. if a config changed,\n// we must include it) and possible optimizations (e.g. we sent a config, but it was not changed).\nfunc (s *XDSServer) compareDiff(\n\tcon *Connection,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n\tsotwRes model.Resources,\n\tdeltaRes model.Resources,\n\tdeleted model.DeletedResources,\n\tusedDelta bool,\n\tdelta model.ResourceDelta,\n\tincremental bool,\n) {\n\tcurrent := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(w.TypeUrl).LastResources\n\tif current == nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: resources initialized\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif deltaRes == nil && deleted == nil && len(sotwRes) == 0 {\n\t\t// TODO: it suspicious full is never nil - are there case where we should be deleting everything?\n\t\t// Both SotW and Delta did not respond, nothing to compare\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tnewByName := slices.GroupUnique(sotwRes, (*discovery.Resource).GetName)\n\tcurByName := slices.GroupUnique(current, (*discovery.Resource).GetName)\n\n\twatched := w.ResourceNames\n\n\tdetails := fmt.Sprintf(\"last:%v sotw:%v delta:%v-%v\", len(current), len(sotwRes), len(deltaRes), len(deleted))\n\twantDeleted := sets.New[string]()\n\twantChanged := sets.New[string]()\n\twantUnchanged := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, c := range current {\n\t\tn := newByName[c.Name]\n\t\tif n == nil {\n\t\t\t// We had a resource, but SotW didn't generate it.\n\t\t\tif watched.Contains(c.Name) {\n\t\t\t\t// We only need to delete it if Envoy is watching. Otherwise, it may have simply unsubscribed\n\t\t\t\twantDeleted.Insert(c.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if diff := cmp.Diff(c.Resource, n.Resource, protocmp.Transform()); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Resource was modified\n\t\t\twantChanged.Insert(c.Name)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// No diff. Ideally delta doesn't send any update here\n\t\t\twantUnchanged.Insert(c.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor _, v := range sotwRes {\n\t\tif _, f := curByName[v.Name]; !f {\n\t\t\t// Resource is added. Delta doesn't distinguish add vs update, so just put it with changed\n\t\t\twantChanged.Insert(v.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tgotDeleted := sets.New[string]()\n\tif usedDelta {\n\t\tgotDeleted.InsertAll(deleted...)\n\t}\n\tgotChanged := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, v := range deltaRes {\n\t\tgotChanged.Insert(v.Name)\n\t}\n\n\t// BUGS\n\textraDeletes := sets.SortedList(gotDeleted.Difference(wantDeleted))\n\tmissedDeletes := sets.SortedList(wantDeleted.Difference(gotDeleted))\n\tmissedChanges := sets.SortedList(wantChanged.Difference(gotChanged))\n\n\t// Optimization Potential\n\textraChanges := sets.SortedList(gotChanged.Difference(wantChanged).Difference(knownOptimizationGaps))\n\tif len(delta.Subscribed) > 0 {\n\t\t// Delta is configured to build only the request resources. Make sense we didn't build anything extra\n\t\tif !wantChanged.SupersetOf(gotChanged) {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s unexpected resources: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, wantChanged.Difference(gotChanged))\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Still make sure we didn't delete anything extra\n\t\tif len(extraDeletes) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s unexpected deletions: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, extraDeletes)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tif len(extraDeletes) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s unexpected deletions: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, extraDeletes)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(missedDeletes) > 0 && !incremental {\n\t\t\t// We can skip this if we are incremental; this expects us to only send a partial list. So these are not actually deletes\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed deletions: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, missedDeletes)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(missedChanges) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed changes: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, missedChanges)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(extraChanges) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif usedDelta {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed possible optimization: %v. deleted:%v changed:%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, extraChanges, len(gotDeleted), len(gotChanged))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed possible optimization: %v. deleted:%v changed:%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, extraChanges, len(gotDeleted), len(gotChanged))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc applyDelta(message model.Resources, resp *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse) model.Resources {\n\tdeleted := sets.New(resp.RemovedResources...)\n\tbyName := map[string]*discovery.Resource{}\n\tfor _, v := range resp.Resources {\n\t\tbyName[v.Name] = v\n\t}\n\tres := model.Resources{}\n\tfor _, m := range message {\n\t\tif deleted.Contains(m.Name) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif replaced := byName[m.Name]; replaced != nil {\n\t\t\tres = append(res, replaced)\n\t\t\tdelete(byName, m.Name)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tres = append(res, m)\n\t}\n\tfor _, v := range byName {\n\t\tres = append(res, v)\n\t}\n\treturn res\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8684,"cache_read_input_tokens":67077,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":8684},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012A4jXk3dbU1E64unk5WjkD","session_id":"7a942838-f197-4b2d-b026-89189f05a14f","uuid":"93d13391-8a25-400e-afb8-a4e3c0126831"}

Based on the updates field, generators may\n// choose to send partial or even no response if there are no changes.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\n\tif w == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\n\tif gen == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tt0 := time.Now()\n\n\t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\n\t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\n\t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\n\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\n\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\n\tvar logFiltered string\n\tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !con.proxy.IsProxylessGrpc() {\n\t\tlogFiltered = \" filtered:\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\n\t\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,\n\t\t\tResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tres, logdata, err := gen.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)\n\tinfo := \"\"\n\tif len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {\n\t\tinfo = \" \" + logdata.AdditionalInfo\n\t}\n\tif len(logFiltered) > 0 {\n\t\tinfo += logFiltered\n\t}\n\tif err != nil || res == nil {\n\t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: SKIP%s for node:%s%s\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, info)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdefer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()\n\n\tresp := &discovery.DiscoveryResponse{\n\t\tControlPlane: ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),\n\t\tTypeUrl:      w.TypeUrl,\n\t\t// TODO: send different version for incremental eds\n\t\tVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,\n\t\tNonce:       nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),\n\t\tResources:   xds.ResourcesToAny(res),\n\t}\n\n\tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\n\tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\n\n\tptype := \"PUSH\"\n\tif logdata.Incremental {\n\t\tptype = \"PUSH INC\"\n\t}\n\n\tif err := xds.Send(con, resp); err != nil {\n\t\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\n\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v\",\n\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase !req.Full:\n\t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\",\n\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\tdebug := \"\"\n\t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\t// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.\n\t\t\tdebug = \" nonce:\" + resp.Nonce + \" version:\" + resp.VersionInfo\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%v%s%s\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res),\n\t\t\tutil.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc ResourceSize(r model.Resources) int {\n\t// Approximate size by looking at the Any marshaled size. This avoids high cost\n\t// proto.Size, at the expense of slightly under counting.\n\tsize := 0\n\tfor _, r := range r {\n\t\tsize += len(r.Resource.Value)\n\t}\n\treturn size\n}\n\n// xdsNeedsPush checks for the common cases whether we need to push or not.\nfunc xdsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) (needsPush, definitive bool) {\n\tif proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel {\n\t\t// Not supported for ztunnel\n\t\treturn false, true\n\t}\n\tif req == nil {\n\t\treturn true, true\n\t}\n\t// If none set, we will always push\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\treturn true, true\n\t}\n\n\t// We can not definitively say if we need to push or not based on generic checks, so we will push based\n\t// on the specific typeURL checks.\n\treturn false, false\n}\n\n// waypointNeedsPush checks if an incremental push is needed for CDS and LDS which normally do a full push.\nfunc waypointNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n\t// Waypoint proxies needs to be pushed for LDS and CDS on kind.Address changes.\n\t// Waypoint proxies have a matcher against pod IPs in them. Historically, any LDS change would do a full\n\t// push, recomputing push context. Doing that on every IP change doesn't scale, so we need these to remain\n\t// incremental pushes.\n\t// This allows waypoints only to push LDS on incremental pushes to Address type which would otherwise be skipped.\n\n\t// Waypoints need CDS updates on kind.Address changes\n\t// after implementing use-waypoint which decouples waypoint creation, wl pod creation\n\t// user specifying waypoint use. Without this we're not getting correct waypoint config\n\t// in a timely manner\n\treturn model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address)\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3295,"cache_read_input_tokens":77906,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3295},"output_tokens":18,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_012A4jXk3dbU1E64unk5WjkD","session_id":"7a942838-f197-4b2d-b026-89189f05a14f","uuid":"9bb4f48d-aefb-4201-b0b2-b82063193d54"}
